Name: AK1A PacketCluster
Function: The original packet DX cluster software
Radio Interaction: Serial TNC on RF; PTT via TNC
Notes: Historic; largely superseded by DXSpider/AR-Cluster.

Name: APRS Messenger
Function: APRS keyboard messaging over sound-card HF modes (GMSK, PSK, MFSK) plus APRS-IS
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in/out; PTT (serial or via AGW soundmodem)
Notes: G4HYG. Brings APRS to HF using soundcard modems rather than 1200-baud VHF.

Name: aprs.fi (mobile app)
Function: APRS map, messaging and beaconing companion to the aprs.fi service
Radio Interaction: BLE KISS TNC (e.g. Mobilinkd TNC3) for off-grid transmit/receive of position beacons
Notes: Distinct from the display-only aprs.fi website (which does not touch a radio).

Name: aprsd
Function: APRS-IS server that can IGate traffic heard on RF
Radio Interaction: Serial TNC for RF igating; PTT via TNC when gating to RF
Notes: WA4DSY; older Linux IGate/server.

Name: APRSdos
Function: The original APRS program that created the mode
Radio Interaction: Serial TNC (KISS/TNC2); GPS in; PTT via TNC
Notes: WB4APR Bob Bruninga; the ancestor of all APRS software.

Name: APRSdroid
Function: APRS position reporting, station map display and messaging from a phone
Radio Interaction: Built-in AFSK soundcard TNC (audio in/out to an HT), or KISS TNC / Direwolf via Bluetooth/USB; PTT via VOX/cable
Notes: By Georg Lukas; the standard Android APRS app, works with an HT and audio cable or an external TNC.

Name: APRSISCE/32 (APRSIS32)
Function: Full APRS client: mapping, messaging, tracking, digipeater and IGate
Radio Interaction: KISS/AGW TNC over serial or TCP; soundcard modem via AGW; GPS in; PTT via TNC
Notes: Actively used; portable version runs on Windows Mobile handhelds.

Name: APRSpoint
Function: APRS mapping client (built around Microsoft MapPoint)
Radio Interaction: Connects to a serial/KISS TNC to view and transmit APRS; PTT via TNC
Notes: Legacy; required MapPoint, so mostly historical now.

Name: APRSPro
Function: APRS mapping, messaging and tracking
Radio Interaction: External TNC via audio/BLE for RF transmit/receive; also APRS-IS
Notes: iOS APRS client (APRSPro Ultimate).

Name: aprx
Function: Lightweight RX/TX IGate and APRS/AX.25 digipeater daemon
Radio Interaction: KISS serial/TCP TNC, or soundmodem/Dire Wolf; PTT via TNC
Notes: Runs on very small hardware; popular fill-in digi/igate.

Name: BPQ32 / LinBPQ
Function: Packet-switch node, BBS and Winlink RMS gateway software networking radios into AX.25/NET-ROM/Telnet backbones
Radio Interaction: Drives KISS/soundcard TNCs and PACTOR modems on connected radios; PTT via TNC; links radio to network
Notes: By John Wiseman G8BPQ; backbone software for packet nodes and radio-linked Winlink gateways.

Name: DIGI_NED
Function: Configurable APRS digipeater with query/response features
Radio Interaction: Via AGWPE or KISS TNC; PTT via TNC
Notes: PE1DNN; legacy but flexible APRS digi engine.

Name: DIXPRS
Function: Python APRS internet-gateway and digipeater
Radio Interaction: KISS TNC (serial) or soundcard modem to radio
Notes: Peer to the listed aprx/DIGI_NED

Name: DXSpider / AR-Cluster / CC Cluster
Function: DX spotting cluster node; RF-capable via AX.25
Radio Interaction: KISS/AGW TNC when fed by RF (mostly telnet today); PTT via TNC
Notes: Historically packet-RF fed; still connects over AX.25 in some networks.

Name: EasyTerm
Function: AGW-based multi-connection packet terminal for BBS/node access
Radio Interaction: Via AGWPE/UZ7HO soundmodem or AGW-capable TNC; PTT via TNC
Notes: Popular simple terminal for reaching packet BBSs.

Name: F6FBB (FBB)
Function: Full-featured packet mailbox/BBS with message forwarding
Radio Interaction: Hardware TNC (KISS/host) or AGWPE; PTT via TNC
Notes: Classic worldwide packet BBS software.

Name: FBB / LinFBB
Function: Classic packet-radio bulletin board system with mail forwarding (can forward to/from Winlink)
Radio Interaction: Hardware or sound-modem (KISS) TNC for AX.25 packet; PTT via TNC
Notes: By F6FBB; still run on legacy packet networks.

Name: GP (GraphicPacket)
Function: BayCom graphical packet terminal
Radio Interaction: Baycom modem/soundcard through TFPCX/FlexNet; PTT via port line
Notes: Legacy European packet terminal.

Name: iBCNU
Function: APRS position reporting and messaging
Radio Interaction: External TNC for RF; APRS-IS over network
Notes: Legacy iOS APRS app.

Name: javAPRS
Function: APRS display and feed handling; early web-embeddable APRS
Radio Interaction: Via TNC or APRS-IS feed
Notes: Legacy; part of the javAPRS/javAPRSSrvr family (server component is IS-only).

Name: JNOS
Function: KA9Q-NOS derivative: AX.25/NET-ROM/IP router, BBS, and Winlink/AMPRNet gateway
Radio Interaction: KISS or AGW TNC, or soundmodem/Dire Wolf; PTT via TNC
Notes: Very common for Winlink and packet BBS/node stations.

Name: KA9Q NOS (NET/NOS)
Function: Original amateur TCP/IP-over-packet stack with node/BBS features
Radio Interaction: KISS TNC; PTT via TNC
Notes: Phil Karn KA9Q; ancestor of JNOS/TNOS/AMPRNet.

Name: KISSet
Function: Terminal program for KISS-only TNCs with scripting
Radio Interaction: KISS serial TNC (e.g. NinoTNC) or soundmodem; PTT via TNC
Notes: Built with modern KISS hardware (NinoTNC) in mind.

Name: LinPac
Function: Ncurses AX.25 terminal with mail and macros
Radio Interaction: Kernel AX.25 attached to a serial TNC or soundmodem; PTT via TNC
Notes: Classic Linux packet terminal.

Name: Linux AX.25 (ax25-tools / ax25-apps + kernel stack)
Function: Kernel AX.25 networking plus utilities (kissattach, mkiss, axcall, call, axlisten, listen)
Radio Interaction: Attaches a KISS TNC or soundmodem to the kernel; PTT via TNC
Notes: Foundation many Linux node/BBS/terminal setups build on.

Name: MacAPRS
Function: APRS mapping/messaging for the Macintosh
Radio Interaction: Serial TNC; GPS in; PTT via TNC
Notes: Sproul brothers; legacy but historically important.

Name: MSYS
Function: DOS packet-radio bulletin board / mailbox with message forwarding
Radio Interaction: TNC via serial (AX.25 over radio)
Notes: WB5BBW; historic packet PBBS not represented among the listed BBS suites

Name: Outpost Packet Message Manager (Outpost PMM)
Function: Email-style front end for exchanging packet messages with any BBS/PBBS or TNC personal mailbox; tactical addressing, forms
Radio Interaction: Drives a packet TNC (KISS, AGWPE, or hardware) for AX.25; PTT via the TNC
Notes: outpostpm.org. Heavily used in ARES/RACES EmComm packet nets.

Name: PinPoint APRS
Function: APRS tracking/messaging with built-in offline vector maps
Radio Interaction: KISS or TNC2 serial TNC, network (TCP) TNC, NMEA GPS; PTT via TNC
Notes: Modern lightweight client; also connects to APRS-IS.

Name: PocketPacket
Function: Full APRS client with a built-in software TNC
Radio Interaction: Software TNC encodes/decodes 1200-baud AFSK through the device audio to a VHF radio; also external KISS TNC
Notes: One of the first soft-TNC APRS apps for iPhone.

Name: PoPT
Function: AX.25 packet terminal supporting KISS over TCP/serial, AXIP/UDP, and Linux AX.25 devices
Radio Interaction: KISS TNC or soundmodem; PTT via TNC
Notes: Cross-platform multi-session terminal.

Name: QtTermTCP
Function: Multi-session KISS/AGW/host terminal and BPQ connection client
Radio Interaction: KISS or AGW TNC (incl. Dire Wolf) over serial/TCP; PTT via TNC
Notes: G8BPQ. Common front-end for BBS/node access.

Name: SARTrack
Function: APRS mapping tailored to search-and-rescue teams; tracking and messaging
Radio Interaction: KISS/AGW TNC or Dire Wolf; GPS in; PTT via TNC
Notes: New Zealand origin; can use Dire Wolf as a virtual TNC.

Name: TNOS
Function: NOS derivative BBS/gateway with AX.25/IP
Radio Interaction: KISS TNC; PTT via TNC
Notes: Sibling of JNOS.

Name: TSTHOST (The Station Terminal)
Function: Multi-connect packet terminal with mailbox
Radio Interaction: Hardware TNC in host mode (or software TNC via TFPCX); PTT via TNC
Notes: DG3DBI; widely used legacy DOS packet terminal.

Name: U2APRS
Function: Soundcard-AFSK APRS tracker/messenger
Radio Interaction: AFSK audio via cable to radio; PTT via VOX or cable/serial
Notes: JA7UDE; transmits APRS without a hardware TNC.

Name: UI-View32 (and UI-View16)
Function: APRS mapping, messaging, digipeater and IGate
Radio Interaction: Radio via TNC in KISS, AGWPE, or BPQ host mode (or serial TNC); PTT handled by the TNC
License: Free (formerly donationware; released free after G4IDE SK)
Notes: Historically the most popular Windows APRS client; huge add-on ecosystem (PMap, WXtoIMG feeds).

Name: URONode
Function: AX.25 node front-end / user gateway
Radio Interaction: Kernel AX.25 attached to KISS TNC or soundmodem; PTT via TNC
Notes: Modern Linux node built atop ax25-tools.

Name: W0RLI MailBox (PBBS)
Function: One of the original packet-radio BBS/mailbox programs
Radio Interaction: TNC via serial (AX.25 over radio)
Notes: Hank Oredson W0RLI; foundational PBBS lineage

Name: WinAPRS
Function: Original Windows APRS mapping/messaging application
Radio Interaction: Serial TNC (KISS or TNC2 command mode); GPS in; PTT via TNC
Notes: Sproul brothers (APRS pioneers); MacAPRS and X-APRS are siblings.

Name: WinPack
Function: Full-featured Windows packet terminal with mail handling and 7plus
Radio Interaction: Hardware TNC in host mode or via AGWPE; PTT via TNC
Notes: G4IDE (also author of UI-View); classic Windows packet terminal.

Name: WINTNC
Function: Multiuser/multiport terminal driver for KISS soundcard modems
Radio Interaction: KISS TNC/soundmodem; PTT via TNC
Notes: Niche Windows multi-session terminal.

Name: Xastir
Function: APRS receive/plot, messaging, IGate and limited digipeating; many map formats
Radio Interaction: Kernel AX.25, serial TNC, or AGW/Dire Wolf; GPS in; PTT via TNC
Notes: Veteran *nix APRS station software.

Name: XRouter (XRPi / XRLin / XRWin)
Function: Multiprotocol packet switch/node (AX.25, NET/ROM, TCP/IP) that can host Winlink access
Radio Interaction: KISS TNCs and sound modems; keys PTT for AX.25 packet
Notes: By Paula G8PZT; alternative to BPQ for packet networking.

Name: XRouter / XRPi
Function: Multi-protocol packet node/router (AX.25, NET-ROM, IP, TCP)
Radio Interaction: KISS/AGW TNC or soundmodem; PTT via TNC
Notes: G8PZT; feature-rich node used across networks.

Name: YAAC (Yet Another APRS Client)
Platforms: Java — Windows / macOS / Linux / Raspberry Pi
Function: APRS client, RF-Internet gateway (IGate), and AX.25 digipeater
Radio Interaction: KISS or AGW TNC (incl. Dire Wolf) over serial/TCP; serial GPS; PTT via TNC
Notes: KA2DDO. Strong OpenStreetMap rendering; scriptable.

Name: Band Master
Function: Telnet DX-cluster client with graphical band map and click-to-tune
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control via Omni-Rig
Notes: VE3NEA; integrates with DX Atlas and Faros

Name: CC User (VE7CC / AR-User)
Function: Spotting client for CC Cluster, AR-Cluster and DXSpider with filtering and band map
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control (Omni-Rig) + logger integration
Notes: Lee Sawkins VE7CC; the standard client for CC Cluster (server already listed, client was not)

Name: CQ/X
Function: Mobile/rover contest logger for QSO parties with GPS county tracking
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control (freq/mode)
Notes: Aimed at mobile state-QSO-party operation.

Name: CT
Function: Pioneering contest logger that defined networked contesting
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control (later versions), CW keying via serial/parallel port
Notes: By K1EA. Historically dominant in the 1990s.

Name: CTESTWIN
Function: Multi-contest logger supporting many JA and DX contests
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control + CW keying
Notes: JR8PPG; widely used in Japan alongside zLog

Name: DXLog.net
Function: Contest logger supporting 500+ contests, SO2R, digital
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control, PTT, CW/voice keying, SO2R, band data
Notes: Descended from the Win-Test code lineage; free since 2019.

Name: DXTelnet
Function: Telnet DX-cluster access with sound alerts, band map and DX 'robots'
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control, click-to-tune
Notes: Fabrizio Sartori IK4VYX

Name: GenLog
Function: General + 200+ contest logger
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control (freq/mode read), CW keying
Notes: By W3KM. Long-running free contest/general logger.

Name: HamRacer
Function: Lightweight free contest logger
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control
Notes: Listed among current free contest loggers.

Name: MINOS
Function: VHF/UHF/microwave contest logger
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control
Notes: By G0GJV. VHF-oriented.

Name: N1MM Logger+
Function: World's most popular contest logger for CW/SSB/digital; networked multi-op, SO2R, bandmap, real-time scoring, call history
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control (freq/mode), PTT keying, CW keying via serial/WinKeyer, band data output; sound-card digital handed to external engines (MMTTY/fldigi/WSJT-X)
Notes: Successor to the legacy N1MM Logger Classic. Uses its own CAT plus optional Omni-Rig.

Name: N1MM Logger+ Spectrum Display
Function: Contest logger whose Spectrum Display window shows a panadapter/waterfall from supported radios' spectrum data or an external SDR, with click-to-tune bandmap.
Radio Interaction: CAT control of the transceiver for frequency/mode; ingests native spectrum data (Icom/Flex/etc.) or SDR I/Q and tunes the rig from the waterfall.
Notes: The free contest standard; spectrum window is the in-scope panadapter feature.

Name: N3FJP Contest Log suite
Function: Family of per-contest loggers plus Field Day Log
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control (freq/mode), CW keying, integrates WSJT-X
Notes: By N3FJP; sold as a suite alongside AC Log.

Name: NA
Function: Classic DOS contest logger
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control, CW keying via serial/parallel
Notes: By K8CC. Part of the DOS-era CT/NA/TR trio.

Name: QARTest
Function: HF/VHF contest logger with integrated digital decoding
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control, PTT, CW keying, sound-card RTTY/PSK/CW decode
Notes: By IK3QAR. Popular in Europe.

Name: RCKLog
Function: SSB/CW contest logging and administration with TRX and packet control
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control, CW/PTT keying
Notes: By DL4RCK/DF3CB heritage; German contest tool.

Name: RCKRtty
Function: RTTY contest logging/operating program supporting full contest exchange automation.
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control plus the MMTTY engine for sound-card/FSK RTTY and PTT.
Notes: By Walter Kraus DL4RCK. Uses MMTTY as its modem.

Name: RoverLog
Function: VHF+ contest logger, popular with rovers
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control via Hamlib
Notes: By N1MU. Multiplatform.

Name: RTTY by WF1B
Function: Classic combined RTTY terminal + contest logger; the standard RTTY-contest program of the 1990s.
Radio Interaction: Drives hardware TNC/terminal units (AEA PK-900, MFJ-1278, HAL, AMT-1, etc.) and later sound-card modems; CAT rig control.
Notes: By Ray Ortgiesen WF1B. Legacy, still fondly referenced in RTTY contesting.

Name: RttyRite
Function: RTTY/PSK operating window built into WriteLog contest software; can drive multiple decode engines and hardware TUs.
Radio Interaction: CAT via WriteLog; sound-card AFSK or FSK/PTT keying; MMTTY/2Tone plug-ins or external terminal units.
Notes: Part of WriteLog (W5XD/K5DJ). Supports remote-side modem deployment for remote stations.

Name: RUMped
Function: Mac contest logging companion to RUMlog
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control, CW/PTT keying
Notes: By DL2RUM; part of the RUMlog ecosystem.

Name: SD (Super Duper)
Function: Fast single-op HF contest logger; SDX (RTTY) and SDV (VHF) variants
Radio Interaction: CAT frequency/mode reading, CW keying
Notes: By Paul O'Kane EI5DI. Renowned for fast keyboard logging.

Name: Skookum Logger
Function: Native Mac contest logger
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control, CW/PTT keying via WinKeyer, band data
Notes: By K1GQ. The leading Mac-native contest logger.

Name: so2sdr
Function: SO2R contest logger with SDR bandmap
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control via Hamlib, CW keying (WinKeyer/cwdaemon), SDR audio for bandmap
Notes: Linux SO2R-focused contest logger.

Name: TACLog
Function: UK/European contest logger
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control, CW keying
Notes: Classic RSGB-era contest logger.

Name: tlf
Function: Console-based contest logger that sends CW and can run RTTY.
Radio Interaction: CAT/PTT via Hamlib; CW keying via cwdaemon (serial/LPT or WinKeyer); RTTY via gMFSK/fldigi.
Notes: Logging program included here because it keys CW on the radio through cwdaemon and drives RTTY engines.

Name: TR Log
Function: Classic DOS contest logger, ancestor of TR4W
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control, CW/PTT keying via serial/parallel port
Notes: By N6TR. Still occasionally run in DOS/DOSBox.

Name: TR4W (TR for Windows)
Function: Windows port of TR Log; 180+ contests, SO2R, bandmap
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control, PTT, CW keying, band output
Notes: Based on N6TR's TR Log.

Name: Tucnak
Function: VHF contest logger with networking and skeds
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control via Hamlib, CW keying, sound-card for meteor-scatter/talk
Notes: By OK1ZIA.

Name: Win-Test
Function: Networked HF/VHF contest logger favored by big-gun stations and DXpeditions
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control, PTT, CW/voice-memory keying, SO2R switching, band decoder output
Notes: By F5MZN/F6FVY. DXLog.net was forked from an earlier shared codebase.

Name: WriteLog
Function: Contest logger with strong built-in RTTY/PSK support
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control, PTT, sound-card RTTY/PSK TX/RX (built-in via MMTTY/2Tone/RITTY), CW keying
Notes: By W5XD. Long the standard for serious RTTY contesting.

Name: YFKtest
Function: Console contest helper with Cabrillo export
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control via Hamlib, CW keying
Notes: By DJ1YFK.

Name: zLog
Function: Full-featured JA and international contest logger with networked multi-op synchronization
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control + CW keying (serial/USB/parallel) + PacketCluster
Notes: Very popular Japanese contest logger (zLog for Windows); regional gap in the list

Name: 2Tone
Function: Free dual-path RTTY decode engine noted for strong weak-signal performance; widely run as a second RTTY decoder alongside MMTTY.
Radio Interaction: Decodes transceiver audio via sound card (receive-decode; TX handled by the host logger).
Notes: By David Wicks G3YYD. Integrates with N1MM Logger+, WriteLog, etc. RTTY-only.

Name: AndFlmsg
Function: Android port of the fldigi/flmsg NBEMS suite supporting MFSK, RTTY, PSK, THOR, Olivia and CW for message exchange.
Radio Interaction: Audio in/out to the radio via acoustic coupling or a wired interface; VOX/interface PTT.
Notes: By the fldigi/NBEMS team. Transmits and receives digital modes through phone audio.

Name: cocoaModem
Function: Mac multimode modem implementing RTTY, PSK, MFSK, Hellschreiber, CW, SITOR and HF-FAX.
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in/out; CAT and PTT/FSK keying via serial.
Notes: By Kok Chen W7AY. Open-source; last updated ~2012 but still used on older Macs.

Name: CW Decoder (mobile)
Function: Real-time Morse decoder apps that copy CW to text, often with BPF, AGC and noise-blanker DSP.
Radio Interaction: Microphone/audio input from a receiver's audio.
Notes: Generic entry for the many similarly named store apps (e.g. 'CW Decoder', 'Morse Code Reader'); receive-only via audio.

Name: CW Player
Function: CW keyboard sender and trainer that transmits Morse and can decode received CW.
Radio Interaction: Transmits CW via sound-card tone or keys the rig; PTT via COM.
Notes: By Patrick Lindecker F6DQM. Companion to his MultiPSK work.

Name: CW Skimmer
Function: Multi-channel Morse (CW) decoder/analyzer that simultaneously decodes every CW signal across the receiver passband, extracts callsigns and plots them on a waterfall using a Bayesian decoder; feeds spots via a built-in telnet server.
Radio Interaction: Takes SDR I/Q (SoftRock/QS1R/RX-888 etc.) or sound-card audio in; controls receiver tuning via CAT/Omni-Rig; outputs decoded-signal telnet spots.
Notes: By Alex Shovkoplyas VE3NEA / Afreet Software (dxatlas.com). ~$75. The original desktop skimmer; also drives the Reverse Beacon Network via Aggregator.

Name: CW Skimmer Server
Function: Headless multi-band version of CW Skimmer that decodes CW on several full bands at once from one wideband SDR and serves spots over telnet.
Radio Interaction: Wideband SDR I/Q input (QS1R, RX-888, Perseus, Red Pitaya, etc.); telnet spot output to loggers / RBN Aggregator.
Notes: VE3NEA / Afreet. Backbone of many RBN CW nodes. Originally QS1R-only; now supports several wideband SDRs.

Name: CW Studio
Function: Morse practice, training and (Pro) audio-decode app that can decode CW from sound.
Radio Interaction: Microphone audio input from a receiver (Pro decoder).
Notes: Free with a paid PRO audio decoder; primarily a trainer that can copy off-air CW.

Name: cwdaemon
Function: Background daemon that receives text over a UDP network socket and keys Morse, used by Linux contest loggers.
Radio Interaction: Keys CW and PTT via serial (DTR/RTS) or parallel port using libcw; a WinKeyer variant drives K1EL hardware.
Notes: Used by tlf, xtlf, CQRLOG. Turns typed text into keyed CW on the rig.

Name: CwGet
Function: Sound-card Morse decoder that converts received CW audio to on-screen text with a tuning spectrum.
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in from the receiver; optional CAT.
Notes: By UA9OSV, DXSoft. Shareware; pairs with CwType for TX and links to loggers like Swisslog.

Name: CWSL_DIGI
Function: Runs many parallel FT8/FT4/RTTY/WSPR decoders across multiple bands by tapping CW Skimmer Server's shared-memory I/Q, reporting to PSK Reporter / RBN.
Radio Interaction: Reads the wideband SDR stream shared by CW Skimmer Server; network spot upload.
Notes: By Alex Ranaldi (github.com/alexranaldi/CWSL_DIGI). Needs CW Skimmer Server + CWSL bandwidth plugin running.

Name: CwType
Function: CW keyboard/terminal that transmits Morse typed from the keyboard, with macros and prosigns.
Radio Interaction: Keys CW and PTT via COM/LPT lines or generates keyed sound-card side-tone.
Notes: By UA9OSV, DXSoft. Free; typically paired with CwGet for full transceive.

Name: Digital Master 780 (DM780)
Function: Multimode component of Ham Radio Deluxe for CW, RTTY, PSK, MT63, SSTV and more, with a SuperSweeper multi-signal decode view.
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in/out; CAT via HRD Rig Control; PTT via COM/VOX/CAT; supports FSK RTTY.
Notes: Ham Radio Deluxe (older HRD versions were free). Tight logbook + rig-control integration.

Name: DroidRTTY
Function: Encodes and decodes amateur RTTY with a tuning waterfall and 20 TX macros.
Radio Interaction: Audio coupling to the radio — phone mic/speaker or a wired/Wolphi-Link interface for RX audio and AFSK TX; VOX or interface PTT.
Notes: By Wolphi (DK5WL). ~$7-9 on Google Play; pairs with the WolphiLink interface.

Name: EXTFSK / XMMT.ocx
Function: Helper add-on that provides true FSK and PTT keying for MMTTY-based programs; XMMT.ocx is the MMTTY ActiveX modem control used by host loggers.
Radio Interaction: Keys FSK and PTT over COM/LPT or USB-serial adapter lines to the transceiver.
Notes: Companion to MMTTY (JE3HHT). Essential glue for FSK RTTY with USB-serial adapters in N1MM+/WriteLog/WinWarbler.

Name: Flwkey
Function: Control program for K1EL WinKeyer CW keyers
Radio Interaction: Keys CW to the rig through a WinKeyer over serial/USB; handles PTT
Notes: Fl* suite CW companion

Name: gMFSK
Function: Early Linux multimode modem (RTTY, CW, MFSK, PSK, Hell, THROB) — the codebase that fldigi grew from.
Radio Interaction: Sound-card (OSS/ALSA) audio in/out; Hamlib CAT/PTT.
Notes: Legacy; largely superseded by fldigi but still packaged in some distros and used by tlf.

Name: GRITTY
Function: Single-signal RTTY decoder built on RTTY-Skimmer technology; statistical squelch prints text only when error probability is low, so it emits little garbage during silence.
Radio Interaction: Decodes 3 kHz of transceiver audio via sound card (receive-only decoder).
Notes: VE3NEA / Afreet (dxatlas.com/gritty). Popular as a second-decoder in N1MM Logger+ since 2015.

Name: Ham Morse
Function: CW decoder and code trainer that copies received Morse to text.
Radio Interaction: Microphone/audio input from a receiver's speaker.
Notes: By Black Cat Systems (Chris Smolinski KC2WACG). Paid App Store app.

Name: HamComm
Function: Decode/encode RTTY, CW, ASCII and AMTOR via a simple serial-port comparator modem
Radio Interaction: comparator hardware modem on serial port; audio from RX; PTT
Notes: Classic 1990s program; the 'HamComm interface' became a de-facto standard emulated by RadioRaft, JVFAX, etc.

Name: Hamscope
Function: Free multimode terminal for PSK31, RTTY, CW, MFSK and packet with a tuning waterfall.
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in/out; PTT/CAT via COM.
Notes: Legacy program still in occasional use; light-weight alternative to fldigi.

Name: HotPaw Morse Decoder
Function: Translates received Morse/CW audio to text using DSP signal analysis.
Radio Interaction: Microphone/line-in audio from a receiver.
Notes: By HotPaw Productions. Universal iOS app with a macOS version.

Name: Iambic Master
Function: Trainer/utility to practice accurate paddle sending using a K1EL WinKeyer or compatible device.
Radio Interaction: Connects to a WinKeyer whose paddle input and keyed output are wired to the rig; keys via the WinKeyer.
Notes: K1EL Systems. Primarily send-practice, but drives the same keyer that keys the radio.

Name: IZ8BLY RTTY
Function: Baudot RTTY terminal
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio, or FSK/PTT via COM port
Notes: By IZ8BLY

Name: K1EL WinKeyer host software (WK / WKdemo / WK3tools)
Function: Configuration and terminal software for K1EL WinKeyer USB devices — set speed, weighting, load message memories and send from the keyboard.
Radio Interaction: The WinKeyer hardware keys CW and asserts PTT to the transceiver over its key/PTT lines; app talks to it over USB-serial.
Notes: By K1EL Systems (Steve Elliott K1EL). WinKeyer is the standard host-controlled CW keyer used by most loggers.

Name: K3NG CW Keyer
Function: Open-source Arduino Morse keyer firmware with memories, contest features and WinKeyer 1.0/2.0 emulation.
Radio Interaction: Keys CW and PTT lines to the transceiver; presents a USB WinKeyer/host interface to logging software; some builds do PTT/CAT sequencing.
Notes: By Anthony Good K3NG (Radio Artisan). Runs on many DIY/commercial keyer boards (NanoKeyer, Funtronics FK-10, etc.).

Name: MixW
Function: Multimode sound-card program (RTTY, CW, PSK, MFSK, packet, SSTV, Hell) with an integrated logbook and DX-cluster.
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in/out; CAT rig control; PTT/FSK/CW keying via COM.
Notes: By Nick Fedoseev UT2UZ / Denis Nechitailov UU9JDR. Long-running shareware; MixW4 is the current line.

Name: MMTTY
Function: The de-facto standard sound-card RTTY terminal and decode/encode engine, used stand-alone and as the RTTY engine inside most contest loggers.
Radio Interaction: Sound-card AFSK on RX/TX; true FSK and PTT keying via COM/LPT or (with EXTFSK) USB-serial; optional CAT.
Notes: By Makoto Mori JE3HHT (MM Hamsoft). Engine embedded in N1MM+, WriteLog/RttyRite, WinWarbler, RCKRtty, etc.

Name: MMVARI
Function: Multi-mode sound-card program/engine for RTTY, PSK31/63, FSK and MFSK with multi-channel decode.
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in/out; PTT and CAT via COM.
Notes: JE3HHT (MM Hamsoft). Alternate RTTY/PSK engine option in N1MM Logger+.

Name: Morse Expert
Function: Probabilistic (Bayesian) CW decoder that copies hand-sent and machine CW to text, tolerant of imperfect timing.
Radio Interaction: Takes receiver audio via sound card (Windows) or microphone/audio (Android).
Notes: By VE3NEA (same author as CW Skimmer). Receive-decode only.

Name: MRP40
Function: Sound-card Morse decoder that copies received CW to text and transmits CW from the keyboard, with a mini-logbook.
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in for decode; CW keying and PTT via serial/USB (or sound-card side-tone).
Notes: By Polar-Electric (polar-electric.com). Shareware; also usable through SDR rigs like FlexRadio.

Name: PocketDigi
Function: Handheld multimode modem for PSK31, RTTY, CW, MFSK and Hell on early PDAs.
Radio Interaction: PDA sound in/out to the radio; PTT via serial.
Notes: By Vojtech Bubnik OK1IAK. Legacy PocketPC-era software still cited for portable digimode use.

Name: PSKer
Function: Live BPSK31 and RTTY45 modem with tune, transmit and log
Radio Interaction: Device microphone/sound-card audio in; transmit via speaker/audio out (VOX) or wired
Notes: By KE7SCH; universal iPhone/iPad build, no extra hardware needed

Name: RBN Aggregator
Function: Collects decoded spots from one or more CW/RTTY Skimmer or Skimmer Server telnet ports and uploads them to the Reverse Beacon Network.
Radio Interaction: Reads Skimmer telnet output (which is itself decoding a radio/SDR) and relays to RBN; can re-serve a local telnet feed.
Notes: Reverse Beacon Network project. Required to become an RBN node; end of the skimming chain rather than direct radio contact.

Name: RiTTY
Function: High-performance sound-card RTTY decoder (v4.5) tuned for weak-signal copy, frequently benchmarked against 2Tone/GRITTY/MMTTY.
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio from the receiver.
Notes: By Brian Beezley K6STI. Modern Windows successor to his classic DOS RITTY.

Name: RITTY (DOS)
Function: Early DSP sound-card RTTY program for DOS; historically important weak-signal RTTY decoder.
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in; serial/parallel FSK/PTT keying.
Notes: K6STI. Legacy but still referenced; predecessor of the Windows RiTTY.

Name: RST Decoder / RST Decoder Pro
Function: Simple Morse decoder that copies CW picked up by the phone to text; Pro adds features.
Radio Interaction: Microphone audio from a receiver.
Notes: Free base app plus a paid Pro version on the App Store.

Name: RTTY Skimmer Server
Function: Wideband multi-channel RTTY decoder that skims all RTTY signals across whole bands and posts spots to telnet/RBN.
Radio Interaction: Wideband SDR I/Q input; telnet spot output.
Notes: VE3NEA / Afreet. Very CPU-intensive (far more than CW Skimmer). Powers RBN RTTY spotting.

Name: THROB / THROBX
Function: Transmit/receive the THROB and THROBX MFSK-style keyboard chat modes
Radio Interaction: audio (sound card) + PTT (VOX/serial)
Notes: Lionel Sear G3PPT; early soundcard digital-mode program still supported inside MultiPSK/fldigi

Name: TrueTTY
Function: Sound-card terminal for RTTY plus PSK31/63, AMTOR/FEC, MFSK, Throb and packet.
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in/out; PTT and FSK keying via COM; CAT tuning.
Notes: By Sergei Podstrigailo UA9OSV, DXSoft (dxsoft.com). Shareware.

Name: winkeydaemon
Function: cwdaemon-compatible driver that lets Linux loggers key CW through a K1EL WinKeyer.
Radio Interaction: Sends WinKeyer serial commands so the WinKeyer keys CW/PTT to the transceiver.
Notes: By Nate Bargmann N0NB. Bridges tlf/CQRLOG to real WinKeyer hardware.

Name: WinWarbler
Function: Sound-card operating program for PSK31/63/125, RTTY and CW with a voice keyer and QSO logging integration.
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in/out; RTTY via MMTTY engine (AFSK or FSK); PTT/CW keying via COM; CAT through DXLab Commander.
Notes: By Dave Bernstein AA6YQ, part of the DXLab Suite (interoperates with Commander/DXKeeper/SpotCollector).

Name: Zakanaka
Function: Free multimode operating program (PSK, RTTY, CW) with rig control and a logging bridge.
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in/out; CAT rig control; PTT via COM.
Notes: Legacy sound-card multimode utility; used with external logging programs.

Name: acarsdec
Function: Multi-channel VHF ACARS decoder
Radio Interaction: SDR (RTL-SDR/Airspy/SDRplay/SoapySDR) or audio
Notes: JSON output; common utility-monitoring decoder in the same class as dump1090/DSD+

Name: AIS-catcher
Platforms: Windows / macOS / Linux / Raspberry Pi / Android
Function: Dual-band AIS receiver with multiple decoding models and built-in web UI
Radio Interaction: SDR (RTL-SDR/Airspy/HackRF)
Notes: NMEA out over UDP/HTTP/TCP; marine counterpart to the listed SeaTTY/NAVTEX tools

Name: ARDOP (ardopc / ardopcf)
Function: Open-source Amateur Radio Digital Open Protocol sound-card HF modem / virtual TNC used by Winlink and Pat
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in/out; PTT via CAT/rigctl, serial RTS/DTR, or GPIO
Notes: Successor concept to WINMOR; bundled with Winlink Express and supported natively by Pat.

Name: DroidPSK
Function: Encode/decode PSK31 (and BPSK/QPSK variants) on a phone or tablet
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio via mic/speaker or a WolphiLink interface; PTT via VOX/interface
Notes: By Wolphi; part of the Wolphi Droid* mobile digital-modes suite.

Name: DSCdecoder
Function: Commercial DSC decoder for MF/HF/VHF Digital Selective Calling signals
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in from the receiver
Notes: By COAA; paired product line with NAVTEX/weatherfax decoders.

Name: dumphfdl
Function: Multichannel HF Data Link decoder (ACARS/ADS-C/CPDLC over HF)
Radio Interaction: SDR; can slave tuning to a receiver
Notes: szpajder; modern SDR HFDL decoder, distinct from the listed PC-HFDL

Name: dumpvdl2
Function: VDL Mode 2 message decoder and protocol analyzer
Radio Interaction: SDR (RTL/Airspy/SoapySDR)
Notes: szpajder; successor to vdlm2dec

Name: gnuais / gnuaisgui
Function: Decode AIS from sound card or SDR
Radio Interaction: audio (sound card) or SDR

Name: ION2G (Ion2G)
Function: Modern ALE 2G/4G controller plus MIL-STD-188-110 serial-tone modem and STANAG-5066 data; aimed at hams, MARS and SHARES
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control for scanning/tuning, sound-card audio for ALE and data waveforms, PTT keying via CAT/serial
Notes: Marketed as an easier-to-set-up ALE package; active development (ion2g.app).

Name: JAERO
Function: Demodulates/decodes Inmarsat AERO ACARS, ADS-C and CPDLC on L-band C-channels
Radio Interaction: SDR or audio from an L-band downconverter
Notes: By Jonti

Name: MARS-ALE
Function: MIL-STD-188-141A/FED-STD-1045A ALE controller with sounding, scanning, LQA and messaging tailored for MARS/CFARS/SHARES nets
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control (frequency/mode/scan), sound-card audio in/out for ALE tones, PTT keying via CAT/RTS/DTR
Notes: By Steve Hajducek N2CKH; the MARS-oriented sibling of PC-ALE. Distributed through the n2ckh.com MARS-ALE forum.

Name: MS-DMT (MIL-STD Data Modem Terminal)
Function: Serial-tone data-modem terminal implementing MIL-STD-188-110A and FED-STD-1052 waveforms (75-2400 bps) for MARS point-to-point and broadcast nets
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in/out for serial-tone waveforms, CAT rig control, PTT keying via CAT/serial
Notes: By N2CKH; companion to MARS-ALE for follow-on messaging and DLP file transfer.

Name: PC-ALE (PCALE)
Function: Software ALE controller emulating MIL-STD-188-141A / FED-STD-1045A for amateur HF ALE networks and channel scanning
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control for scan/tune, sound-card audio in/out for 8-FSK ALE tones, PTT via CAT/serial
Notes: Originally by Charles Brain G4GUO; the first widely available PC ALE controller (2000). Distributed by HFLINK (hflink.com).

Name: PC-HFDL
Function: Decodes HFDL (HF ACARS / ARINC-635) aircraft data-link messages
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in from an HF SSB receiver
Notes: By Charles Brain G4GUO; the classic dedicated HFDL decoder (5-minute demo, ~$35).

Name: PlanePlotter
Function: Decode and plot ADS-B/Mode-S and ACARS aircraft on a map
Radio Interaction: SDR or hardware receiver via audio/serial/network
Notes: COAA

Name: rtl-ais
Function: Lightweight command-line AIS decoder (both channels)
Radio Interaction: RTL-SDR
Notes: Classic RTL-SDR AIS utility

Name: ShipPlotter
Function: Receive and plot AIS traffic on charts
Radio Interaction: sound card or serial/USB AIS receiver
Notes: COAA

Name: Sigmira
Function: SDR/receiver decoder for PSK31, RTTY, CW, STANAG-4285, HFDL, MIL-STD-188-141A (M141 ALE), FSK, and Japanese Slot Machine
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in from a conventional receiver, or direct control of RTL-SDR / RFSpace / SDRplay SDR hardware
Notes: By Steve Harlow. Receive/decode focused (incl. ALE monitoring); can drive several SDR front-ends directly.

Name: TIVAR
Function: Data-modem terminal for MIL-STD-188-110 / STANAG serial-tone waveforms; also used to decode Shortwave Radiogram (MFSK/data) broadcasts
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in/out for data waveforms; used with an SSB HF radio
Notes: Used in MARS/utility circles and by SW Radiogram listeners; sound-card based.

Name: VARA FM
Function: Sound-card FM data modem (9600-bps-class) for Winlink over VHF/UHF FM radios
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in/out through an FM transceiver; PTT via CAT/serial/VOX
Notes: EA5HVK's FM counterpart to VARA HF; used for fast Winlink FM email.

Name: VarAC
Function: Keyboard-to-keyboard HF chat, beaconing, file/position exchange and short messaging over the VARA modem
Radio Interaction: Drives the VARA HF/FM soundcard modem; CAT rig control and PTT via Hamlib/serial
Notes: Fast-growing modern HF data app built on top of VARA HF.

Name: WINMOR
Function: Legacy sound-card HF virtual-TNC modem for Winlink radio email
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in/out; PTT keying via CAT/serial
Notes: By Rick Muething KN6KB; retired in favor of ARDOP/VARA but still seen on older stations.

Name: YaDD (Yet Another DSC Decoder)
Function: Decodes GMDSS Digital Selective Calling (DSC) distress/routine traffic and logs MMSIs
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in from an MF/HF/VHF receiver on DSC calling channels
Notes: Companion to YaND; widely used for DSC monitoring.

Name: YaND
Function: 'Yet Another NAVTEX Decoder' – decodes 518/490 kHz NAVTEX maritime safety broadcasts from the sound card.
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio input; receive-only.
Notes: Focused on NAVTEX (SITOR-B FEC); commonly grouped with fax/WEFAX listening tools.

Name: YaND (Yet Another NAVTEX Decoder)
Function: Decodes and logs 518/490 kHz NAVTEX broadcasts with station identification
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in from an MF/HF receiver tuned to NAVTEX channels
Notes: By Dirk Claessens; the standard hobby NAVTEX decoder (ndblist.info).

Name: Allmon3
Function: Modern web dashboard for monitoring and controlling AllStarLink nodes
Radio Interaction: controls app_rpt/Asterisk nodes (which key radios)
Notes: Successor to the listed Allmon2/Supermon

Name: AllStarLink (app_rpt / Asterisk)
Function: Asterisk-based repeater/node linking network (VoIP) with EchoLink/IRLP bridging
Radio Interaction: USB CM108 sound fob (or URI/DINAH/repeater interface) for radio audio + COS/PTT keying
Notes: WB6NIL origin; the app_rpt engine.

Name: ambed
Function: AMBE transcoding server for XLX/URF reflectors (D-STAR AMBE ↔ DMR AMBE)
Radio Interaction: Uses AMBE3000 hardware (DVstick/ThumbDV farms) to transcode radios' DV audio between modes
Notes: Required for cross-mode transcoding on XLX.

Name: AMBEServer
Function: Serves a hardware AMBE (DV3000/ThumbDV) dongle over the network to DV clients/bridges
Radio Interaction: Exposes the hardware AMBE vocoder used to encode/decode radio DV audio
Notes: Used by DVSwitch, DUDE-Star, etc.

Name: Analog_Bridge
Function: Converts analog (AllStar/EchoLink) audio to/from digital vocoded streams
Radio Interaction: AMBE transcoding between analog radio audio and DV networks
Notes: DVSwitch component.

Name: APRSGateway
Function: Aggregates APRS/position data from a hotspot's DV modes to APRS-IS
Radio Interaction: Passes GPS/APRS frames embedded in the radio's DV traffic
Notes: G4KLX; part of MMDVM suite.

Name: ASL3 (AllStarLink v3)
Function: Current AllStarLink release packaged as Debian apt packages on modern Asterisk
Radio Interaction: Same CM108/URI audio + PTT/COS keying of node radios
Notes: Modernized AllStar distribution.

Name: BlueDV AMBE / BlueDV Server
Function: Helper that serves an AMBE hardware dongle to BlueDV clients
Radio Interaction: Exposes the AMBE3000 hardware vocoder over the network
Notes: PA7LIM.

Name: BlueDV for Android
Function: Mobile DMR/D-STAR/Fusion client using a Bluetooth AMBE dongle or network
Radio Interaction: Bluetooth/USB AMBE dongle for hardware vocoding, or network-only into DV reflectors
Notes: PA7LIM.

Name: BlueDV for Windows
Function: Desktop DMR/D-STAR/Fusion client with dashboard, driving a DVMega/MMDVM modem or an AMBE dongle
Radio Interaction: Serial to a DVMega/BlueStack modem (RF) or an AMBE3000 dongle (ThumbDV/DVstick) for vocoding + PC audio
Notes: David PA7LIM. Popular for DVMega and ThumbDV setups.

Name: BrandMeister server (bmaster)
Function: Master server software behind the BrandMeister DMR network
Radio Interaction: Routes DMR traffic/talkgroups for connected hotspots and repeaters
Notes: Operated by the BrandMeister team.

Name: Buster
Function: Native macOS D-STAR client to work reflectors/gateways
Radio Interaction: Uses a ThumbDV/DVstick hardware AMBE vocoder + Mac audio into the D-STAR network
Notes: Jeremy McDermond NH6Z.

Name: cBridge (c-Bridge)
Function: Commercial DMR conference bridge/network controller for MOTOTRBO/DMR networks
Radio Interaction: Interconnects DMR repeaters and routes talkgroup RF traffic
Notes: Rayfield Communications; common in early DMR networks.

Name: ClearNode app
Function: Companion app to control ClearNode AllStar/EchoLink/DMR gateway hotspots
Radio Interaction: Manages links/keying on the ClearNode node that connects to node radios/handhelds
Notes: Node-Ventures (Gerry Filby); Supermon-like control.

Name: D-RATS
Function: Text chat, file transfer, position reporting/mapping and forms over D-STAR low-speed data (and Winlink bridging)
Radio Interaction: Serial connection to the D-STAR radio's DV-data port; drives the transceiver's digital data channel
Notes: Maintained community fork on GitHub; can relay to Winlink with lzhuf.

Name: DAPNET (MMDVM POCSAG paging)
Function: Decentralized amateur paging network; transmits POCSAG pages through MMDVM hotspots/transmitters
Radio Interaction: Feeds POCSAG data to MMDVMHost which keys the modem's RF to page receivers
Notes: Hampager/DAPNET; POCSAG TX via hotspot.

Name: DGIdGateway
Function: Yaesu Fusion DG-ID gateway mapping DG-ID groups to different Fusion reflectors
Radio Interaction: Handles the radio's C4FM stream by DG-ID for reflector selection
Notes: G4KLX.

Name: DMRGateway
Function: Multi-network DMR gateway letting one MMDVM system reach up to ~6 DMR networks (BrandMeister, DMR+, TGIF, HBLink) plus an XLX at once with talkgroup rewrites
Radio Interaction: Sits between MMDVMHost and the DMR networks; routes the radio's DMR frames
Notes: G4KLX. Ships in Pi-Star/WPSD.

Name: DMRHub
Function: Modern self-hostable DMR master server with web UI and OpenBridge
Radio Interaction: Acts as DMR master routing hotspot/repeater DMR traffic
Notes: USA-RedDragon; Go implementation.

Name: DMRlink
Function: IPSC (Motorola MOTOTRBO) DMR linking/bridging server
Radio Interaction: Links DMR repeaters over IPSC; bridges their RF traffic
Notes: N0MJS; pairs with IPSC_Bridge/HB_Bridge.

Name: DroidStar
Function: Multi-mode DV client for DMR, D-STAR (REF/XRF/DCS), YSF/FCS, P25, NXDN, M17 reflectors and AllStar (as IAX2)
Radio Interaction: Software AMBE/IMBE vocoder over UDP; can also drive an MMDVM modem directly (direct mode) as a hotspot/standalone transceiver
Notes: Doug McLain AD8DP. Very widely used.

Name: DStarGateway
Function: Modernized, actively-maintained rewrite of ircDDBGateway for D-STAR
Radio Interaction: Same gateway role for D-STAR repeaters/hotspots
Notes: Geoffrey F4FXL.

Name: DStarRepeater
Function: D-STAR repeater/node controller for homebrew hardware (GMSK node adapter, DVAP, MMDVM)
Radio Interaction: Drives the D-STAR RF modem/node adapter and feeds ircDDBGateway
Notes: G4KLX; pairs with ircDDBGateway.

Name: DUDE-Star
Function: Desktop multimode client (D-STAR, Fusion, DMR, NXDN, P25, M17, AllStar)
Radio Interaction: Built-in software IMBE/AMBE vocoder or an AMBE3000 USB dongle; PC sound card audio
Notes: AD8DP; predecessor/sibling of DroidStar.

Name: DudeShield
Function: Community fork of DUDE-Star for DMR/YSF/D-STAR network access
Radio Interaction: Software or hardware AMBE vocoder into DV reflectors
Notes: OpenShield fork.

Name: DummyRepeater
Function: Turns a PC + AMBE dongle into a D-STAR 'radio' node with no RF, working through ircDDBGateway
Radio Interaction: Uses a DV Dongle/ThumbDV AMBE hardware vocoder plus PC sound for voice into the D-STAR network
Notes: G4KLX; a way to be on D-STAR without an RF radio.

Name: DV4mini control software
Function: Control panel for the DV4mini USB hotspot stick (D-STAR, DMR, Fusion, C4FM, APRS)
Radio Interaction: USB to the DV4mini RF stick (10mW) that keys/receives DV to your handheld and links to reflectors
Notes: Legacy but still in use.

Name: DVAPTool
Function: Control software for the DVAP Dongle personal D-STAR hotspot
Radio Interaction: USB to the DVAP RF stick (~10mW GMSK) that keys/receives D-STAR to your handheld and links via ircDDBGateway
Notes: AA4RC; classic D-STAR hotspot.

Name: DVSwitch DIAL image
Function: Prebuilt image bundling the DVSwitch server tools
Radio Interaction: Same DVSwitch bridging/AMBE roles
Notes: Turnkey DVSwitch install.

Name: DVSwitch Mobile
Function: Phone client for analog + digital modes that pairs with a user's DVSwitch Server
Radio Interaction: Full-duplex audio + low-latency PTT to a DVSwitch Server, which bridges to DV/analog networks and AMBE
Notes: DVSwitch.org / N4IRS & N4IRR.

Name: DVSwitch Server (Analog_Bridge + MMDVM_Bridge)
Function: Server suite that bridges AllStar/analog audio to DMR/D-STAR/YSF/P25/NXDN and between DV networks
Radio Interaction: Transcodes/keys DV streams; bridges to RF via AllStar node radios or MMDVM; uses AMBE (dongle or ambed)
Notes: Core of many cross-mode bridges.

Name: DVTool (DV Dongle)
Function: Client for the DV Dongle to work D-STAR reflectors/gateways from a PC
Radio Interaction: USB DV Dongle provides the AMBE hardware vocoder; PC sound for voice into D-STAR
Notes: Robin Cutshaw AA4RC.

Name: EchoLink (Windows)
Function: VoIP linking to EchoLink nodes/conferences; runs as single-user or as a sysop node/link
Radio Interaction: In sysop mode keys a node radio via sound card + serial/VOX PTT with COS detect; single-user is network-only
Notes: Jonathan Taylor K1RFD. Huge installed base.

Name: EchoLink for Android
Function: Mobile EchoLink client to connect to nodes/conferences
Radio Interaction: Network VoIP into RF EchoLink node radios
Notes: Official app.

Name: EchoLink for iOS
Function: iPhone/iPad EchoLink client
Radio Interaction: Network VoIP into RF EchoLink node radios
Notes: Official app.

Name: EchoLink Proxy
Function: Relay allowing EchoLink use behind firewalls/NAT
Radio Interaction: Passes EchoLink traffic to/from node radios on the network
Notes: Helper for the EchoLink network.

Name: EchoLinux
Function: Open-source EchoLink-compatible client
Radio Interaction: Network VoIP into RF EchoLink nodes
Notes: Legacy cross-platform EchoLink client.

Name: FCS reflector (FCS00x)
Function: Fusion Connect System reflector network (persistent Fusion rooms)
Radio Interaction: Routes C4FM Fusion traffic from connected radios/hotspots
Notes: Reached from YSFGateway/openSPOT.

Name: FreeDMR
Function: Community DMR server network stack (HBLink-derived) with self-care and dynamic talkgroups
Radio Interaction: Routes DMR frames from connected hotspots/repeaters
Notes: Simon G7RZU. Hotspots connect via MMDVM_Bridge/DMRGateway.

Name: HAMVOIP
Function: AllStar-based distribution/image for Pi with its own tools
Radio Interaction: CM108 sound fob audio + PTT/COS to node radio
Notes: Popular RPi AllStar image; diverged from ASL.

Name: HBLink3
Function: Homebrew DMR master/peer server for building DMR networks and bridges (Homebrew/MMDVM protocol)
Radio Interaction: Aggregates and routes DMR frames from many hotspots/repeaters; conference bridges/talkgroups
Notes: Cort N0MJS lineage; Python.

Name: HBmonitor
Function: Web dashboard/monitor for HBLink/FreeDMR servers
Radio Interaction: Displays and helps manage DMR traffic routed for connected radios
Notes: Companion to HBLink.

Name: Icom RS-MS3A / RS-MS3W
Function: Software for D-STAR Terminal/Access-Point mode, letting an Icom DV radio reach the gateway over the internet
Radio Interaction: Connects the D-STAR radio (USB) to the internet gateway for callsign routing/reflectors
Notes: Icom terminal/AP-mode helper.

Name: IPSC2 / DMRplus master
Function: DMR+ (DMRplus) master/reflector server software
Radio Interaction: Routes DMR traffic and reflectors for connected repeaters/hotspots
Notes: DMR+ team; hotspots connect via DMRGateway 'DMR Plus'.

Name: ircDDBGateway
Function: D-STAR gateway providing callsign routing (ircDDB), DPlus/REF, DExtra/XRF, DCS and XLX reflector linking, CCS7 and D-RATS
Radio Interaction: Connects a D-STAR repeater/hotspot (DStarRepeater/MMDVMHost/DVAP) to the D-STAR network
Notes: G4KLX. The classic D-STAR gateway.

Name: IRLP
Function: Internet Radio Linking Project node software linking repeaters/simplex nodes and reflectors
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio + serial/parallel-port PTT and COS via the IRLP interface board to the radio
License: Free (proprietary node software; requires IRLP board)
Notes: Dave Cameron VE7LTD. Requires purchased board/node number.

Name: m17-cxx-demod / M17 tools
Function: Reference M17 modulator/demodulator command-line tools
Radio Interaction: audio (sound card) baseband to/from radio; USRP/network
Notes: Core M17 Project tools; complements the listed mvoice/mrefd/M17Gateway/OpenRTX

Name: M17Gateway
Function: Gateway linking an MMDVM hotspot to the open M17 reflector network
Radio Interaction: Relays the radio's M17 (Codec2) stream to M17 reflectors
Notes: G4KLX; M17 is the ham-developed open DV mode.

Name: MMDVM cross-mode gateways (YSF2DMR, YSF2NXDN, YSF2P25, DMR2YSF, DMR2NXDN, NXDN2DMR)
Function: Transcode/bridge one DV mode to another so, e.g., a Fusion radio can work DMR talkgroups
Radio Interaction: Convert the radio's DV frames between protocols mid-stream
Notes: G4KLX/community 'MMDVM_CM'; enables cross-mode operation on Pi-Star/WPSD.

Name: MMDVM firmware
Function: Firmware for MMDVM modem boards/hotspots that modulates/demodulates DMR/D-STAR/YSF/P25/NXDN/M17/POCSAG
Radio Interaction: Directly generates and receives the RF DV baseband; talks to MMDVMHost over serial
Notes: G4KLX plus MMDVM_HS variants (F1RMB, N7TAE, EA7EE builds).

Name: MMDVM_Bridge
Function: Connects to DMR/D-STAR/YSF/P25/NXDN networks and decomposes each into a mode-agnostic stream for bridging
Radio Interaction: Network side of DVSwitch; bridges the radio's DV frames between systems
Notes: DVSwitch component; used with Analog_Bridge.

Name: MMDVMCal
Function: Calibration utility to tune an MMDVM modem's TX/RX offset, deviation and levels
Radio Interaction: Talks to the modem over serial and keys test carriers/patterns on RF for tuning
Notes: G4KLX. Essential when setting up a new modem board.

Name: MMDVMHost
Function: The core host program that ties an MMDVM/DVMega modem to DMR/D-STAR/YSF/P25/NXDN/M17/POCSAG networks; underlies Pi-Star and WPSD
Radio Interaction: Serial/USB link to the MMDVM modem; the modem does the RF; also drives Nextion/OLED and PTT via the modem
Notes: Jonathan Naylor G4KLX. The engine of nearly every MMDVM hotspot.

Name: mrefd (M17 Reflector)
Function: Reflector server for the open M17 digital-voice network
Radio Interaction: Routes M17 (Codec2) streams from hotspots/clients
Notes: N7TAE / M17 Project.

Name: mvoice
Function: Reference desktop client for the open M17 network
Radio Interaction: Software Codec2 vocoder over the network into M17 reflectors (can pair with hotspot for RF)
Notes: N7TAE / M17 Project.

Name: NXDNGateway
Function: NXDN gateway linking a hotspot to NXDN reflectors (e.g., NXCore/TGIF)
Radio Interaction: Routes the radio's NXDN frames to/from the network
Notes: G4KLX.

Name: NXDNReflector
Function: Reflector server for NXDN (used by NXCore/TGIF NXDN)
Radio Interaction: Routes NXDN traffic among connected radios/hotspots
Notes: G4KLX.

Name: OP25
Function: APCO-25 (and DMR) receiver/decoder built on GNU Radio
Radio Interaction: Controls RTL-SDR/Airspy front-ends — tunes control channels and follows trunked voice
Notes: Osmocom OP25 / boatbod fork; common on scanner Pis.

Name: OpenDV suite
Function: Packaging/distribution of G4KLX's D-STAR software (ircDDBGateway, DStarRepeater, PCRepeaterController)
Radio Interaction: Same D-STAR repeater/gateway RF/network roles as its components
Notes: Maintained by DL5DI/community.

Name: P25Gateway
Function: P25 Phase-1 gateway connecting a hotspot to P25 reflectors/talkgroups
Radio Interaction: Routes the radio's P25 IMBE stream between MMDVMHost and P25 network
Notes: G4KLX.

Name: P25Reflector
Function: Reflector server for P25 talkgroups
Radio Interaction: Routes P25 IMBE traffic among hotspots/repeaters
Notes: G4KLX.

Name: Peanut
Function: Simple client to reach D-STAR, DMR, Fusion and M17 talk rooms over the internet with no hotspot/dongle
Radio Interaction: Network/DV-reflector linking with software vocoder; gateways into RF DV networks (no local RF)
Notes: PA7LIM. Licensed hams only; validation required.

Name: Pi-Star
Function: Turnkey SD-card image that runs a multi-mode MMDVM digital-voice hotspot or repeater (DMR, D-STAR, YSF/FCS, P25, NXDN, POCSAG) with a web dashboard
Radio Interaction: Hotspot/network linking; drives an MMDVM or DVMega modem over serial/USB which transmits/receives the RF digital-voice signal
Notes: Andy Taylor (MW0MWZ). Long the default hotspot OS; development slowed ~2025 and the community shifted to WPSD.

Name: QnetGateway (g2_ircddb / qnet)
Function: Alternative D-STAR ircDDB gateway supporting DVAP, ThumbDV/DVstick and MMDVM hotspots
Radio Interaction: Links a D-STAR node/hotspot (RF via DVAP/MMDVM, or AMBE dongle) to the D-STAR network
Notes: Tom Early N7TAE.

Name: Qtel
Function: EchoLink client GUI (part of the SvxLink project)
Radio Interaction: Network VoIP into RF EchoLink nodes; PC audio
Notes: Tobias SM0SVX.

Name: SharkRF openSPOT (firmware + web config)
Function: Standalone multi-mode hotspot (DMR, D-STAR, Fusion, NXDN, P25, cross-mode) configured via built-in web interface
Radio Interaction: The device's own RF modem talks to your handheld; firmware links it to DV networks; SharkRF app assists config
Notes: openSPOT/2/3/4 hardware; runs pure-C embedded OS, no Pi-Star.

Name: Supermon / Allmon2 / AllScan
Function: Web dashboards to monitor and control AllStar nodes (connect/disconnect links, key, DTMF)
Radio Interaction: Issues commands to app_rpt that link/key the node's radio
Notes: AllScan is the modern PHP dashboard.

Name: SvxLink server
Function: Repeater controller, simplex node, EchoLink node and networked reflector with voice modules
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio to the transceiver + PTT/COS via GPIO, serial DTR/RTS, or CM108 USB; can key repeater RF
Notes: SM0SVX. Widely used repeater/EchoLink controller.

Name: SvxReflector
Function: Networked reflector interlinking multiple SvxLink nodes
Radio Interaction: Routes audio among SvxLink node radios/repeaters
Notes: Part of the SvxLink project.

Name: thebridge
Function: EchoLink conference-server software for hosting rooms
Radio Interaction: Bridges/conferences EchoLink node-radio audio
Notes: Basis of thelinkbox radio linking.

Name: thelinkbox
Function: Repeater controller and VoIP linking application (separate from EchoLink/IRLP networks)
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio + PTT/COS interface to link radios/repeaters
Notes: Skip Hansen; based on thebridge with radio interface code.

Name: urfd (URF Reflector)
Function: Unified multi-mode reflector (D-STAR, DMR, YSF, NXDN, P25, M17) succeeding XLX
Radio Interaction: Routes/transcodes DV traffic among connected hotspots/repeaters
Notes: N7TAE. Modern XLX replacement.

Name: WinDV / DV-RPTR software
Function: Control/node software for DV-RPTR modem boards (D-STAR)
Radio Interaction: Serial/USB to the DV-RPTR modem which handles D-STAR RF
Notes: Legacy German DV-RPTR hardware.

Name: Wires-X (Yaesu)
Function: Yaesu's node/room software for System Fusion (C4FM) internet linking via HRI-200 or a Portable Digital Node
Radio Interaction: Connects a Fusion radio (via HRI-200 or DR-2X/FT USB) using CAT/USB + sound for RF node operation
Notes: Windows 7+; the official Fusion linking system.

Name: WIRES-X Portable Digital Node (PDN)
Function: HRI-200-less mode of Wires-X using a supported Fusion radio directly as a personal node
Radio Interaction: USB-connects an FT-70/FT3/FTM-series radio; the radio provides RF and vocoder
Notes: Popular low-cost Fusion node.

Name: WPSD (W0CHP-PiStar-Dash)
Function: Actively-maintained next-gen fork of Pi-Star; same multi-mode MMDVM hotspot/repeater roles plus cross-mode, better dashboard and updater
Radio Interaction: Same as Pi-Star: controls MMDVM/DVMega modem over serial/USB for RF DV
Notes: Chip (W0CHP). Now the most-recommended hotspot distribution.

Name: xlxd (XLX Reflector)
Function: Multiprotocol reflector server providing all-mode D-STAR + DMR + YSF interlink with a web dashboard
Radio Interaction: Reflects/routes DV traffic from many hotspots/repeaters; transcodes via ambed
Notes: LX1IQ. XLX modules (A-Z).

Name: YSFGateway
Function: Yaesu System Fusion gateway connecting an MMDVM hotspot to YSFReflector/FCS rooms
Radio Interaction: Relays the radio's C4FM stream from MMDVMHost to Fusion reflectors
Notes: G4KLX; part of the MMDVM gateway suite.

Name: YSFReflector
Function: Reflector/room server for Yaesu System Fusion (C4FM)
Radio Interaction: Routes C4FM streams from Fusion hotspots/repeaters
Notes: G4KLX; the standard YSF room server.

Name: Codec 2
Platforms: Windows, macOS, Linux, Raspberry Pi, embedded
Function: Open-source low-bit-rate (700–3200 bps) speech codec plus reference 'freedv' command-line encode/decode tools; the engine inside FreeDV and M17.
Radio Interaction: CLI tools read/write audio (WAV/stdin) for feeding a rig's sound-card path; library used by radios/apps.
Notes: By David Rowe. Foundational codec; not a full station app by itself but ships the freedv CLI.

Name: FreeDV / freedv-gui
Function: Open-source HF digital voice: compresses speech with Codec 2 and modulates it (700D/700E/1600/2020 etc.) so any SSB rig carries low-bit-rate DV; also has a text/data channel.
Radio Interaction: Two sound cards (radio audio in/out plus speaker/mic); PTT via serial or Hamlib/rigctl; CAT rig control for frequency.
Notes: By David Rowe and contributors; the reference FreeDV application. Companion SM1000 is the hardware version.

Name: mvoice (M17)
Function: M17 digital-voice client using Codec 2 3200/1600; connects to M17 reflectors and can key radios/hotspots.
Radio Interaction: Sound-card/USB audio to a rig or hotspot; network linking of M17 gateways; PTT via interface.
Notes: By the M17 Project. M17 chose Codec 2 as its open codec, tying it to the FreeDV codec family.

Name: QRadioLink
Function: SDR-based transceiver app supporting Codec 2 digital voice plus analog and data modes over VHF/UHF/HF SDR hardware.
Radio Interaction: Directly drives SDR transceivers (audio + I/Q) and keys TX; supports Codec 2 DV.
Notes: By Adrian Musceac YO8RZZ. Brings FreeDV-style Codec 2 voice into an SDR platform.

Name: Aether
Function: Modern native Mac logger with QSO management, awards, Cabrillo/ADIF
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control
Notes: Paid Mac App Store logger.

Name: BBLogger
Function: Free general logging program
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control
Notes: Listed among free ham logging programs.

Name: Cloudlog
Function: Web-based logbook with awards, maps, LoTW/QSL
Radio Interaction: Reads live rig frequency/mode via a CAT bridge (Hamlib/rigctld companion posting to its Radio API)
Notes: By M0OUK; CAT is via a local bridge process.

Name: CommCat
Function: Logging with integrated rig control, cluster and mapping
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control, PTT
Notes: Windows station-management logger.

Name: CQRLOG
Function: Advanced Linux logger (Firebird DB) with cluster, grayline, LoTW/eQSL
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control via Hamlib/rigctld (incl. IP rigs like IC-7300); integrates fldigi/WSJT-X
Notes: By OK2CQR.

Name: DX4WIN
Function: DXer-oriented logging program
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control
Notes: Popular with serious and casual DXers.

Name: DXbase
Function: Legacy DX logging program
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control
Notes: Older Windows/DOS logger still in some shacks.

Name: DXKeeper
Function: Full logging, QSLing, LoTW/eQSL and awards tracking
Radio Interaction: captures freq/mode and tunes rig via DXLab Commander
Notes: DXLab Suite logging component; only Commander and WinWarbler were listed from the suite

Name: DXLab Suite
Function: Free modular station-management suite: DXKeeper (log), Commander (CAT), SpotCollector (cluster), WinWarbler (digital), DXView, PropView, etc.
Radio Interaction: Commander does CAT/PTT rig control; WinWarbler does sound-card PSK/RTTY/CW TX-RX with PTT; modules feed the rig frequency/mode
Notes: By AA6YQ. Modules run standalone or together.

Name: DXtreme Station Log
Function: Commercial general logging program with reporting
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control
Notes: Long-standing commercial logger.

Name: DXView
Function: World map of spots, beam headings, greyline; commands rotators
Radio Interaction: rotator control + rig control via Commander
Notes: DXLab Suite component

Name: Fast Log Entry (FLE)
Function: Very fast keyboard/text logging that converts shorthand to ADIF/Cabrillo
Radio Interaction: None directly - offline text logger; no CAT/audio/PTT (listed here for completeness per the logging category)
Notes: By DF3CB. Included because it is named in the logging set, but it does not itself touch the radio.

Name: Flcluster
Function: Telnet DX-cluster client integrated with the Fl* suite
Radio Interaction: Can QSY the rig by sending spot frequency to flrig/fldigi
Notes: Newer member of the suite

Name: Fllog
Function: Stand-alone logbook server that stores QSOs and serves them over XML-RPC to fldigi/flrig
Radio Interaction: Captures frequency/mode from flrig/fldigi at log time; no direct CAT itself
Notes: Provides a shared log back-end for the suite

Name: Flnet
Function: Net-control check-in roster and member database for running on-air nets
Radio Interaction: Can read current frequency from flrig/fldigi; primarily a net-management database
Notes: Part of the Fl* suite

Name: HAM Office
Function: German-language logging/station suite
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control
Notes: Popular in the DACH region.

Name: Ham Radio Deluxe (HRD)
Function: Comprehensive suite: Logbook, Rig Control, DM-780 digital, Rotator, Satellite tracking
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control, sound-card digital via DM-780 with PTT, CW keying, rotor control
Notes: By HB9DRV originally; free through v5, now paid. Rig Control module runs standalone.

Name: Ham Radio Deluxe - Digital Master (DM780)
Function: Multimode (PSK, RTTY, Olivia, Contestia, DominoEX, MT63, Feld Hell, MFSK, CW, SSTV) integrated with the HRD suite
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in/out; CAT rig control and PTT via HRD Rig Control
Notes: DM780/Digital Master component of Ham Radio Deluxe (HRD Software LLC)

Name: HamLog
Function: Portable field/mobile logger with search, ADIF, QRZ lookup, cloud sync
Radio Interaction: Rig control (freq/mode) via Pignology Piglet/Pigtail WiFi/serial CAT hardware
Notes: By Pignology; CAT requires the Piglet/Pigtail interface.

Name: KLog
Function: Multiplatform Qt logger with awards and DXCC tracking
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control via Hamlib
Notes: By EA4TV.

Name: Log-EQF / Win-EQF
Function: Long-running general logger
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control (frequency/mode)
Notes: Win-EQF is the Windows successor to the DOS LOG-EQF.

Name: Log4OM
Function: Free logging program with integrated CAT control, cluster spotting and click-to-tune bandmap.
Radio Interaction: Controls the transceiver via OmniRig or Hamlib (frequency/mode/split), tunes to cluster spots, and shares CAT with digital apps.
Notes: By Daniele Pistollato IW3HMH; strong CAT + cluster integration.

Name: Log4OM (v1 / v2)
Function: Modern station-management logbook with cluster, awards, cloud sync
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control via Omni-Rig or Hamlib, CW via WinKeyer, integrates external digital (WSJT-X/fldigi)
Notes: By IW3HMH.

Name: Logger32
Function: Lightweight DX logger with bandmap and cluster
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control (auto freq/mode capture) plus rotor control
Notes: By K4CY. Very stable and low-resource.

Name: Logic (LOGic)
Function: Logging program with rig control, awards and mapping
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control
Notes: By Personal Database Applications.

Name: MacLoggerDX
Function: Mac/iOS logging and DX program with built-in rig control for ~100 radios, cluster spotting and click-to-tune.
Radio Interaction: Direct CAT/CI-V control of frequency/mode; tunes the radio to DX-cluster spots and can key/track for satellites.
Notes: A primary Mac rig-control + spotting bridge.

Name: MixW / MixW2 / MixW3
Function: All-in-one multimode (PSK, RTTY, SSTV, MFSK, MT63, Olivia, Packet, CW) with logbook, DX cluster and contest support
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in/out; CAT rig control, PTT, and antenna-rotator control via COM ports
Notes: By Nick Fedoseev UT2UZ / UU9JDR; long-time popular Windows suite, now under RigExpert

Name: MixW4
Function: Cross-platform rewrite of MixW: PSK, RTTY, Olivia, MFSK, DominoEX, CW plus JT65/FT8, with logging, DX cluster, contest tools, KiwiSDR as external RX
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio; CAT/Hamlib rig control and PTT (RigCAT); FSK keying
Notes: By RigExpert; actively developed multi-OS successor to MixW

Name: N3FJP Amateur Contact Log (AC Log)
Function: General logger with DXCC/WAS/WAC/county award tracking
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control (freq/mode), integrates WSJT-X/digital
Notes: Flagship of the N3FJP suite.

Name: N3FJP Amateur Contact Log (ACLog) + Rig Interface/Gateway
Function: General/contest logging suite with a built-in rig-control interface and a network gateway/API other N3FJP and third-party apps use for CAT.
Radio Interaction: Reads/sets radio frequency and mode via built-in CAT (or Hamlib/OmniRig), and its API Gateway relays rig data to other software.
Notes: By Scott Davis N3FJP; the 'ACLog gateway' bridges CAT to companion apps.

Name: ProLog
Function: Legacy general logging program
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control
Notes: By Datamatrix; DOS-era logger.

Name: PstRotator (PstRotatorAZ)
Function: Antenna-rotator control program (AZ/EL) with logger/cluster tracking and satellite support that reads and follows rig frequency.
Radio Interaction: Reads the transceiver's frequency/mode via CAT (Hamlib/OmniRig/direct) to auto-point antennas and, for satellites, sends Doppler CAT tuning to the rig.
Notes: By Codrut Buda YO3DMU; touches the radio via CAT for tracking/Doppler.

Name: RUMlog / RUMlogNG
Function: Free Mac logbook and contest logger with cluster and QSL management
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control (freq/mode), CW/PTT keying
Notes: By DL2RUM.

Name: SpotCollector
Function: Aggregates spots from up to six sources into a real-time local DX database
Radio Interaction: rig control via DXLab Commander; click-to-tune
Notes: DXLab Suite component; the suite is listed but this individual app was not

Name: Swisslog
Function: Comprehensive QSO management, awards, cluster
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control
Notes: By HB9BJS.

Name: Turbo HAMLOG/Win
Function: General-purpose station logbook; the de-facto standard everyday logger in Japan
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control (reads freq/mode)
Notes: Shoichi Hamada JG1MOU; major non-English regional logger

Name: TurboLog 4
Function: DX/contest logging with cluster, mapping, rig and rotor control
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control + rotator + cluster
Notes: German logging suite (distinct from Turbo HAMLOG)

Name: UcxLog
Function: Combined general logbook, contest logger and digital-mode terminal
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control, PTT, sound-card digital (PSK/RTTY/CW), CW keying
Notes: By DL7UCX.

Name: VQLog
Function: HF/VHF/satellite logbook covering all amateur bands
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control
Notes: By EA6VQ.

Name: Wavelog
Function: Modern actively developed fork of Cloudlog
Radio Interaction: Receives live rig freq/mode from a CAT interface/Hamlib companion via its API
Notes: Community fork with faster development cadence.

Name: WinLog32
Function: Freeware DX logger for HF/VHF with DXCC/IOTA/WAZ tracking
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control
Notes: By G0CUZ.

Name: Xlog
Function: Daily and contest logging program
Radio Interaction: Hamlib CAT to read frequency/mode/S-meter over serial/USB
Notes: GTK logger for GNU/Linux.

Name: YPLog
Function: Rig-control-centric logging program for Yaesu/Ten-Tec/Elecraft
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control panel with logging
Notes: By G4HFQ.

Name: AirLink Express
Function: User-friendly PSK, MFSK and RTTY package with logging and macros
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in/out; CAT rig control and PTT via COM port
Notes: Modern-looking free multimode terminal

Name: Bonito RadioCom
Function: DSP multimode decode/encode: RTTY, PSK, FAX, SSTV, weather and analog modes
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in/out; CAT rig control and PTT
Notes: RadioCom 5/6 from Bonito

Name: cocoaModem / cocoaModem 2.0
Function: Multimode modem: RTTY, PSK, MFSK, Feld Hell, CW, SITOR/AMTOR, DominoEX, WEFAX
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in/out; CAT rig control and PTT via serial/USB
Notes: By Kok Chen W7AY; the standard native Mac multimode app

Name: Code332
Function: Multitasking data analyzer, demodulator and decoder for many modes
Radio Interaction: Sound-card / IF audio in (receive decode)
Notes: Part of the professional-decoder lineage related to Sorcerer

Name: Contact-Multi
Function: Multimode PSK, RTTY, FSK, MFSK terminal with logging
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in/out; PTT via COM port
Notes: Lesser-known freeware multimode terminal

Name: DarwinPSK
Function: Early native Mac PSK31 terminal
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in/out; PTT via serial port
Notes: By W7AY; predecessor to cocoaModem

Name: DigiDecoder
Function: Auto-identify, decode and encode SSTV, Packet, RTTY, CW, FT8, FT4, JS8 and Olivia with waterfall
Radio Interaction: Device microphone audio in; encode via speaker audio out
Notes: Recent multimode phone app

Name: DigiPan
Function: Panoramic PSK31/PSK63 program showing the whole waterfall as a clickable dial
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in/out; PTT via COM port or VOX
Notes: By Skip Teller KH6TY and Nick Fedoseev UT2UZ; pioneered the click-to-tune waterfall

Name: EasyDRF
Function: 'Easy Digital Radio Files' – HamDRM application for sending files/images over HF radio.
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in/out; serial-port PTT keying.
Notes: By DazDSP; part of the WinDRM/HamDRM family alongside EasyPal.

Name: Flamp
Function: Amateur Multicast Protocol: blocked, FEC-protected file broadcast/relay with fill requests
Radio Interaction: Transmits/receives through the fldigi modem (sound-card audio + PTT via fldigi)
Notes: By W1HKJ; used to push files/forms to many stations at once

Name: Fldigi
Function: Flagship free multimode soundcard modem: PSK31/63/125, RTTY, MFSK, Olivia, Contestia, DominoEX, Feld Hell, MT63, THROB, CW, FSQ, IFKP, Navtex, WEFAX, plus built-in logging and macros
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in/out for all modes; CAT via built-in Hamlib or via flrig (XML-RPC); PTT via CAT, RTS/DTR, or rigCAT
Notes: By W1HKJ (Dave Freese) et al.; core of NBEMS; runs well headless on Raspberry Pi; de-facto standard for keyboard digital modes and EmComm

Name: Flmsg
Function: ARRL Radiogram / ICS / plaintext / custom form manager and formatter for message handling
Radio Interaction: No direct radio link; hands formatted messages to fldigi which transmits/receives them over the air
Notes: Part of NBEMS; pairs with fldigi + flamp for EmComm traffic

Name: Flwrap
Function: File encapsulation with checksum for error-free transfer of text/binary over radio
Radio Interaction: Prepares files that fldigi transmits/receives (indirect; via fldigi audio + PTT)
Notes: Older companion, largely superseded by flamp for bulk transfer

Name: FSQCall
Function: FSQ (Fast Simple QSO) HF-NVIS selective-calling, chat and message/file exchange
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in/out; PTT via COM port
Notes: By Con Wassilieff ZL2AFP and Murray Greenman ZL1BPU; FSQ also built into fldigi

Name: IZ8BLY Hellschreiber
Function: Feld Hell and Hellschreiber variants encode/decode
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in/out; PTT via COM port
Notes: By IZ8BLY; classic Hell program

Name: IZ8BLY MT63
Function: MT63 mode terminal
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in/out; PTT via COM port
Notes: By IZ8BLY; original popular MT63 implementation

Name: LinPSK
Function: Qt PSK31/PSK63 and RTTY multimode terminal
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in/out; PTT via Hamlib/serial
Notes: Native Linux PSK/RTTY program

Name: multimon-ng
Function: Decodes POCSAG, FLEX, AFSK/APRS (AX.25), DTMF, ZVEI, X10 and more from audio or SDR
Radio Interaction: audio pipe from SDR/receiver (e.g. rtl_fm)
Notes: Fork of multimon; standard building block in SDR decode pipelines; not listed despite rtl_433/dump1090 being present

Name: MultiPSK
Function: Very broad multimode: BPSK/QPSK, PSK63/125, RTTY, MFSK, Feld Hell, THROB, Olivia, Contestia, MT63, ALE, SSTV, HF-FAX, plus utility/marine RX modes (Navtex, ACARS, DGPS, Pactor RX)
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in/out; CAT rig control and PTT via COM port
Notes: By Patrick Lindecker F6CTE; one of the widest mode collections available

Name: N7YG Digital Engine
Function: PSK, RTTY and MFSK modem that integrates with N3FJP logging software
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in/out; PTT via COM port
Notes: By N7YG; digital front-end for N3FJP loggers

Name: PSK31SBW
Function: Original 'PSK31 for SoundBlaster/Windows' terminal
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in/out; PTT via COM port
Notes: By AE4JY; one of the first Windows PSK31 programs

Name: RadioRaft
Function: Automatic recognition and decode of many data modes (Baudot, ARQ/FEC, Packet, CW, utility modes)
Radio Interaction: Audio/comparator interface from the receiver (receive-only decode)
Notes: By François Guillet F6FLT; classic auto-classifying decoder

Name: SIM PSK31 (Sim31 / Sim63)
Function: Lightweight PSK31/PSK63 terminal
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in/out; PTT via COM port
Notes: Small, simple PSK program from the early PSK era

Name: Sorcerer
Function: SDR-style multimode signal decoder (PSK, RTTY and many HF utility/data modes) with add-a-decoder interface
Radio Interaction: Sound-card / IF audio in for decoding (receive-only; no transmit)
Notes: Descended from professional Code300/332-family decoders; popular for utility monitoring

Name: twpsk / tkpsk
Function: PSK31 terminal (Tcl/Tk front end to a soundcard modem)
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in/out; PTT via serial
Notes: Part of the older Linux ham digital toolset

Name: WinDRM
Function: Amateur DRM (HamDRM) program sending digital voice, text and images over HF using COFDM/QAM.
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in/out; serial-port PTT keying.
Notes: By Cesco HB9TLK / Mel Whitten K0PFX group; a precursor to FreeDV's DV approach and to EasyPal-style DRM data.

Name: WinPSK
Function: PSK31 keyboard terminal
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in/out; PTT via COM port
Notes: By Moe Wheatley AE4JY

Name: WinPSKse
Function: Enhanced WinPSK capable of receiving two PSK31 signals simultaneously
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in/out; PTT via COM port
Notes: By AE4JY; 'special edition' evolution of WinPSK

Name: Black Cat Systems iOS decoders (PSK31, Morse Pad, RTTY, SSTV)
Function: Individual PSK31, Morse (CW), RTTY and SSTV decoders with waterfall
Radio Interaction: Device microphone audio in from the radio (receive decode)
Notes: By Black Cat Systems; set device near the radio speaker

Name: FDMDV
Function: Narrow (1.1 kHz) HF digital voice using close-spaced OFDM carriers; works at weaker signal levels than WinDRM
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in/out through an SSB transceiver; PTT via serial/VOX
Notes: HB9TLK's low-bandwidth DV mode; direct ancestor of the FreeDV 1600 waveform.

Name: IZ8BLY Stream
Function: MFSK16 / MFSK8 (plus experimental FEC/PSK/MSK modes)
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in/out; PTT via COM port
Notes: By Nino Porcino IZ8BLY (with ZL1BPU); helped popularize MFSK

Name: Mobilinkd Config app
Function: Configure Mobilinkd Bluetooth-LE KISS TNC parameters
Radio Interaction: BLE link to the hardware TNC, setting audio levels and PTT behaviour that key the radio
Notes: Companion to APRSdroid / aprs.fi and any KISS app.

Name: NinoTNC (N9600A) host utilities
Function: IL2P/AX.25 hardware KISS TNC used with KISSet, BPQ32 or Dire Wolf; firmware/config tools
Radio Interaction: KISS over USB serial; PTT via the TNC to the radio
Notes: TARPN-network TNC; multiple baud rates and FEC modes.

Name: OTWincfg (OpenTracker config, Argent Data)
Function: Configure Argent Data OpenTracker / Tracker2 APRS TNC-trackers
Radio Interaction: Serial link to the hardware tracker that generates the packet audio and keys PTT
Notes: Sets APRS paths, profiles and I/O for the OpenTracker family.

Name: PacFORMS
Function: HTML representation of standardized ICS/served-agency forms; Submit builds a compact text message, recreated on receipt
Radio Interaction: Via Outpost to the packet TNC/radio (no direct radio access itself)
Notes: Companion to Outpost; forerunner of Winlink-style form workflows.

Name: TinyTrak Config (Byonics)
Function: Configure Byonics TinyTrak APRS trackers (paths, beacon timing, smart beaconing)
Radio Interaction: Serial link to the hardware tracker that generates AFSK and keys the radio PTT
Notes: Programs the tracker's on-air behaviour; MT-AIO/all-in-one variants.

Name: TNC-Pi / TNC-X utilities (pitnc_getparams, xtinit)
Function: Read/set KISS parameters (TXDelay, persistence, audio) on TNC-Pi and TNC-X hardware
Radio Interaction: Configures the hardware KISS TNC that keys the radio (audio + PTT)
Notes: Used when building BPQ32/Dire Wolf packet nodes on a Pi.

Name: Alinco Programmer / Clone Utility
Function: Alinco factory clone/programming utilities and per-radio programmers (incl. APK-MD5 for the DMR DJ-MD5) to set memories, CTCSS/DCS and menu options.
Radio Interaction: USB/serial clone read/write via ERW-4C/ERW-7/ERW-8 cables.
Notes: DJ-MD5/DR-MD500 DMR models also programmed via RT Systems and (analog) CHIRP.

Name: Anytone CPS
Function: AnyTone customer programming software for DMR handhelds/mobiles (AT-D868UV, AT-D878UV/UVII/Plus, AT-D578UV III), editing channels, zones, talkgroups, color codes, contacts and roaming.
Radio Interaction: USB read/write of the full DMR codeplug and firmware; radio-and-firmware-revision-specific.
Notes: BTECH DMR-6X2/PRO use an AnyTone-derived CPS. Windows-only; also supported by qdmr/dmrconfig/RT Systems.

Name: AnyTone Firmware/QX Upgrade Tool
Function: AnyTone firmware-loader utility used to update D868/D878/D578 firmware (paired with the CPS codeplug).
Radio Interaction: USB firmware flashing to the radio (boot/upgrade mode).
Notes: Firmware and CPS versions must match; distributed by AnyTone/BridgeCom.

Name: ARC series (BuTel)
Function: Model-specific programming/control for Uniden, AOR and Alinco scanners (ARC536, ARCPatrol, etc.)
