Radio Interaction: serial/USB programming + control
Notes: BuTel Software

Name: Baofeng CPS (factory / VIP)
Function: Baofeng factory programming software (VIP and per-model CPS for UV-5R, BF-888S, UV-82, DM-1701 DMR, etc.) to set memories, CTCSS/DCS and menu options.
Radio Interaction: USB read/write of channels/settings via CH340/FTDI programming cable.
Notes: CHIRP is the more popular tool for Baofeng analog; DMR models (DM-1701/RD-5R) also use dmrconfig/qdmr/OpenGD77.

Name: Baofeng Ola
Function: Official Baofeng Bluetooth programming app for BT-capable models
Radio Interaction: Bluetooth codeplug programming

Name: CHIRP (CHIRP-next)
Function: Free universal memory-channel programming/editor for hundreds of amateur (and some commercial) radios; spreadsheet-style channel editing, import from RepeaterBook/CSV, cross-radio copy/paste.
Radio Interaction: USB/serial clone: reads and writes memory channels and many settings to the radio over a programming cable (CH340/FTDI/Prolific); some radios support live per-channel read/write.
Notes: CHIRP-next (daily/py3 build) is the current version; supports Baofeng, Wouxun, TYT, Retevis, Yaesu, Icom, Kenwood, Alinco, Anytone (analog), Quansheng UV-K5 EEPROM, and many more. The de-facto standard free programmer.

Name: Connect Systems CPS (CS700/CS800/CS580)
Function: Connect Systems customer programming software for their CS700/CS701/CS750/CS800/CS800D DMR and CS580 analog radios.
Radio Interaction: USB read/write of DMR/analog codeplug (channels, contacts, zones).
Notes: Codeplugs (RDT) also editable in N0GSG Contact Manager.

Name: contactsync
Function: Helper that pulls the RadioID.net/DMR-MARC user database and formats DMR contact CSVs for import into various radios' CPS.
Radio Interaction: Indirect: builds the contact list that is then written to the radio via the vendor CPS/codeplug tool.
Notes: Included per task hint; strictly a data-prep helper that feeds codeplug/CPS imports rather than touching the radio itself.

Name: CSI CPS (CS750/751 700/701)
Function: CSI/Connect Systems 700/701 series DMR customer programming software.
Radio Interaction: USB read/write of DMR codeplug (RDT/RDB).
Notes: Codeplugs editable in N0GSG Contact Manager; older but still in service.

Name: dmrconfig
Function: Serge Vakulenko KK6ABQ's command-line utility to read/write DMR codeplugs and edit them as text .conf scripts.
Radio Interaction: USB read/write of the radio codeplug image; applies text-config scripts directly to the radio or an image.
Notes: Now under the OpenRTX org; supports TYT MD-380/390/2017/UV380/UV390/9600, DM-1701, RD-5R, GD-77, AnyTone D868/878, BTECH DMR-6X2, Zastone D900.

Name: editcp / codeplug (Farnsworth)
Function: Dale Farnsworth's open-source DMR codeplug editor (editcp GUI) and underlying 'codeplug' library; edits and format-translates codeplugs.
Radio Interaction: Reads/writes DMR codeplug images and can transfer to supported radios; also converts codeplugs between devices.
Notes: Handy for MD-380/UV380-class radios; also used to migrate codeplugs across models.

Name: Elecraft K3 / KX3 / K4 Utility
Function: Manufacturer configuration and firmware utility for Elecraft K3/K3S, KX3/KX2 and K4 radios.
Radio Interaction: Connects over serial/USB (or network for K4) to read/write radio configuration, memories/macros and update firmware.
Notes: In-scope as it directly programs and configures the transceiver.

Name: EVE / Miklor programming helpers
Function: Assorted small community programmers/driver-and-CPS bundles curated by Miklor for Baofeng/Wouxun/TYT radios.
Radio Interaction: USB/serial read/write of memory channels via CH340/Prolific cables.
Notes: Mostly repackaged factory CPS plus driver guidance; CHIRP is the recommended primary tool.

Name: FreeSCAN
Function: Free programmer for many Uniden scanners
Radio Interaction: USB/serial programming
Notes: Development abandoned; no support for newest DMR/SDS models

Name: G4HFQ Programmers (FTBasicMMO, FTB7800/8800, VX Commander)
Function: Bob Freeth G4HFQ's suite of Yaesu programmers: FTBasicMMO (FT-817/857/897), FTB7800/7900/8800/8900, FTB2800, VX-5/VX-7 Commander, FT-90 Commander, etc.; CSV import/export between radios.
Radio Interaction: Serial/CI-V clone read/write of memory channels and settings.
Notes: Long-standing legacy software still widely used for older Yaesu rigs.

Name: Hytera CPS
Function: Hytera Customer Programming Software for DMR portables/mobiles (PD3/5/6/7 series, MD series, etc.); channels, zones, talkgroups, encryption.
Radio Interaction: USB read/write of the radio codeplug plus a separate tuner/firmware package.
Notes: Version/series-specific; some features (e.g. AES256) require paid unlocking.

Name: Icom CS-xxxx Cloning Software
Function: Icom's per-radio cloning/programming software (CS-9700, CS-7300, CS-705, CS-7100, CS-51, CS-F for LMR, etc.) to set memory channels, repeater lists and menu settings.
Radio Interaction: USB/CI-V/serial clone read/write of the radio's memory and settings image.
License: Free (Icom download for most amateur models)
Notes: Model-specific; e.g. CS-9700 for IC-9700, CS-7300 for IC-7300. Third-party WCS-xxxx (RT Systems) programs the same radios.

Name: Icom RS-MS1A / RS-MS1I
Function: Icom D-STAR companion app that edits repeater/memory lists and DV settings and controls the radio's screen from a phone/tablet.
Radio Interaction: Reads/writes repeater and memory lists and DV/DR settings to the transceiver over USB (OPC cable) or Bluetooth.
Notes: Used with IC-2730, ID-5100, ID-51/52 etc.; effectively on-device codeplug/repeater-list editing plus control.

Name: k5prog / k5prog-win
Function: Command-line tool to read/write the Quansheng UV-K5/UV-K6/UV-5R EEPROM and back up calibration, and flash firmware.
Radio Interaction: USB/serial EEPROM read/write and firmware flashing over the Kenwood-style 2-pin cable.
Notes: Recommended for backing up UV-K5 calibration before flashing custom firmware.

Name: K5TOOL
Function: qrp73's UV-K5/UV-R5 toolkit to read/write EEPROM and flash firmware.
Radio Interaction: USB/serial EEPROM dump/restore and firmware flashing.
Notes: Popular alongside k5prog for the Quansheng UV-K5 hacking scene.

Name: Kenwood KPG series (KPG-D)
Function: Kenwood commercial/LMR programming software (KPG-D1N for NEXEDGE, KPG-134D, KPG-101D, etc.) for conventional and NXDN/DMR business radios.
Radio Interaction: USB/serial read/write of the radio codeplug via KPG programming cables (KPG-22/36/46).
Notes: Dealer/authorized software for Kenwood/EFJohnson land-mobile radios.

Name: Kenwood MCP series
Function: Kenwood factory Memory Control Program (MCP-2A, MCP-4A, MCP-5A, MCP-6A, MCP-D74, MCP-D75, etc.) for editing memory channels, DV/APRS and menu settings.
Radio Interaction: USB/serial read/write of memory channels and configuration to the radio.
Notes: MCP-D74/D75 for TH-D74/D75; MCP-2A for TM-D710/RC-D710. ARFC-D74 (frequency control) is a separate Kenwood control app.

Name: KG-UV Commander
Function: Free third-party programmer (KC8UNJ) for early Wouxun KG-UVD1P / KG-UV2D/3D handhelds.
Radio Interaction: USB/serial read/write of memory channels and settings.
Notes: Legacy but still used; author (Jim Mitchell KC8UNJ) also wrote other free programmers.

Name: md380tools / md380-dfu
Function: Travis Goodspeed's patched-firmware toolchain for the TYT MD-380/390; also reads/writes raw codeplug images and uploads DMR user databases.
Radio Interaction: USB DFU flashing of patched firmware and raw codeplug read/write via md380-dfu.
Notes: Enabled the famous 'promiscuous' firmware and full-callsign DMR user DB on MD-380-class radios.

Name: Motorola CPS (RSS / CPS 2.0 / RadioCentral)
Function: Motorola programming software families: legacy DOS RSS, CPS/CPS 2.0 and cloud-linked RadioCentral for MOTOTRBO DMR and older LMR radios.
Radio Interaction: USB/serial read/write of the radio codeplug (channels, zones, talkgroups, features).
Notes: Common on surplus MOTOTRBO radios used by hams; entitlement/AirTracer tools separate.

Name: N0GSG DMR Contact Manager
Function: DMR codeplug editor (Tom Wheeler N0GSG) with channel/zone/scan-list editing, IntelliPaste, and direct DMR-MARC/RadioID contact import for many DMR radios.
Radio Interaction: Edits vendor codeplug files (RDT/RDB) and imports contacts; codeplug is written to the radio via the vendor CPS.
Notes: Supports Connect Systems CS700/701/800, TYT MD-380/390, Retevis RT3, AnyTone D868/878 and similar; a widely used codeplug wrangler.

Name: Odmaster (TIDRADIO)
Function: Wireless (Bluetooth) and USB codeplug programming for 100+ HT models; GPS repeater import; CHIRP CSV import
Radio Interaction: Bluetooth/USB codeplug programming
Notes: Works across Baofeng/Radioddity/Retevis/Tenway; modern phone-based CPS

Name: OpenGD77 CPS (Community CPS)
Function: Community customer programming software matched to OpenGD77 open firmware; edits and writes codeplugs for radios running OpenGD77.
Radio Interaction: Serial read/write of the OpenGD77 codeplug while the radio is powered on; also flashes firmware.
Notes: For GD-77/77S, RD-5R, DM-1701/1801, TYT MD-UV380/MD-9600, Retevis RT-3S/RT-84/RT-90 running OpenGD77. Factory codeplugs are not compatible.

Name: OpenGD88 CPS
Function: Community customer programming software for the OpenGD88 open firmware on the Radioddity/TYT GD-88 dual-DMR radio.
Radio Interaction: USB read/write of the OpenGD88 codeplug and firmware flashing.
Notes: Sister project to OpenGD77 for the newer GD-88 hardware.

Name: OpenRTX + radio_tool
Function: Open-source firmware for digital ham handhelds plus radio_tool, the utility used to read/write firmware (and codeplug regions) on supported radios.
Radio Interaction: USB DFU/serial flashing of firmware images and codeplug flashing on TYT MD-3x0/UV3x0/9600, GD-77, DM-1801.
Notes: radio_tool is the cross-platform flasher; OpenRTX replaces the stock firmware and its programming model.

Name: ProScan
Function: Programming plus live control and logging for Uniden scanners
Radio Interaction: serial/USB control + codeplug programming

Name: qdmr (and dmrconf)
Function: Hannes Matuschek DM3MAT's universal GUI (qdmr) + CLI (dmrconf) that store DMR config in a human-readable, device-independent format and program many radios.
Radio Interaction: USB read/write of the binary codeplug to the radio; converts the portable config into vendor-specific codeplugs.
Notes: Supports RD-5R, MD-UV390, RT3S, OpenGD77 firmware, AnyTone D868/D878/D878II/D578, and more.

Name: Radioddity CPS
Function: Radioddity per-model programming software for GD-77/RD-5R DMR and GA/GM analog/GMRS radios.
Radio Interaction: USB read/write of channels, zones, DMR contacts/talkgroups.
Notes: GD-77/RD-5R can alternatively run OpenGD77 with the Community CPS.

Name: Retevis / Ailunce CPS
Function: Retevis and Ailunce customer programming software for their analog (RT-series) and DMR (RT3S, RT84, RT90, Ailunce HD1) radios.
Radio Interaction: USB read/write of channels, zones, talkgroups and DMR contacts.
Notes: Many are rebadged TYT/AnyTone radios; CHIRP handles the analog side of most.

Name: RT Systems Radio Programmers
Function: Commercial per-radio programming software suite (ADMS-, WCS-, KRS-, RPS-, DMR- series) with polished spreadsheet UI, band-plan templates, address-book import; one title per radio model.
Radio Interaction: USB/serial read/write of memories and menu settings via RT Systems' own USB cable or the radio's factory cable.
License: Commercial (~$25-50 per model, often bundled with cable)
Notes: Covers Yaesu, Icom, Kenwood, Alinco, Wouxun, Anytone and more. RT Systems also authors several Yaesu-branded ADMS titles under contract.

Name: Scancat-Gold
Function: Multi-brand computer control and logging for scanners and HF receivers
Radio Interaction: CAT/serial control of many receivers and scanners
Notes: Computer Aided Technologies; markets 'supports all scanners'

Name: Tait Programming Application (TPA)
Function: Tait's customer programming application for TP/TM conventional and DMR/P25 land-mobile radios.
Radio Interaction: USB/serial read/write of the radio codeplug/database.
Notes: Tait radios appear on the ham surplus market; TPA/CCTM are the programming tools.

Name: TYT CPS
Function: TYT/Tytera customer programming software for MD-380/390, MD-UV380/390, MD-2017, MD-9600 and analog TH-series.
Radio Interaction: USB read/write of DMR/analog codeplug and contact/zone lists.
Notes: Many TYT DMR models can also be run with OpenGD77/OpenRTX firmware and programmed with qdmr, dmrconfig or N0GSG Contact Manager.

Name: uBITX Manager
Function: Configure calibration, CW, memories and settings on uBITX/BITX transceivers
Radio Interaction: USB serial to the radio's Raduino/Arduino

Name: Uniden Sentinel
Function: Programs Uniden DMA/SDS/BCD scanners; firmware and RadioReference database updates
Radio Interaction: USB (memory/codeplug + firmware)
Notes: Uniden's official programmer; scanner-class receivers absent from the list

Name: UV-K5 Web Flasher / EEPROM Configurator
Function: Browser-based flashers/configurators (e.g. Egzumer 'Flash with a browser', F4HWN/other forks) to load UV-K5 custom firmware and edit settings.
Radio Interaction: WebSerial USB firmware flashing and EEPROM/settings read-write directly from the browser.
Notes: No install needed; used to flash Egzumer/F4HWN/Fagci-spectrum firmware onto the Quansheng UV-K5/K6.

Name: Vertex Standard CE / VX Programming Software
Function: Vertex Standard/Yaesu-LMR customer programming (CE-series, e.g. CE44/CE82) for VX-series commercial radios.
Radio Interaction: Serial/USB read/write of the radio codeplug via FIF-12 / USB interface.
Notes: Used for surplus VX-2200/VX-231/etc. often repurposed by hams.

Name: WCS-xxxx (RT Systems Icom titles)
Function: RT Systems' Icom-specific programmers (WCS-9700, WCS-7300, WCS-705, etc.) with spreadsheet UI for memory channels and menu settings.
Radio Interaction: USB/CI-V read/write of Icom memories and settings.
Notes: Third-party alternative to Icom's factory CS-xxxx cloning software.

Name: Whistler EZ Scan (WS Software)
Function: Programming and management for Whistler scanners
Radio Interaction: USB (codeplug) + firmware

Name: Wouxun CPS (KG-UV series)
Function: Wouxun factory programming software per model (KG-UVD1P, KG-UV9D, KG-935G, KG-Q10, DMR KG-D series).
Radio Interaction: USB/serial read/write of memory channels and settings.
Notes: CHIRP and RT Systems (RPS-KGUVD1P) also support many Wouxun models; free KG-UV Commander covers early models.

Name: Xiegu firmware/config tools (XigFW / G-series updater)
Function: Firmware update and configuration for Xiegu G90/X6100/X6200/G106 transceivers
Radio Interaction: USB firmware flashing + configuration
Notes: Manufacturer flagged in the critic prompt; the similar Lab599 TX-500 updater is analogous

Name: Yaesu ADMS (factory series)
Function: Yaesu factory memory/codeplug programming software (ADMS-7, ADMS-11, ADMS-14, ADMS-M400, ADMS-991, etc.), one per radio.
Radio Interaction: USB/serial clone read/write of memory channels and set-menu settings using SCU-/CT- factory cables.
License: Free (Yaesu download for most current models)
Notes: Naming overlaps with RT Systems ADMS titles; some Yaesu ADMS downloads are RT-Systems-built OEM versions.

Name: Airspy SpyServer
Function: Network IQ-streaming server so a remote SDR# / SDR++ client can use the radio
Radio Interaction: Controls the local Airspy/RTL-SDR hardware and streams tuned IQ over the network
Notes: The standard way to put an Airspy or RTL-SDR on the LAN/Internet for thin clients.

Name: AOR control software (ARC/AR-family)
Function: PC control of AOR wideband receivers (AR-DV1, AR5001D, AR2300, etc.)
Radio Interaction: serial/USB CAT control
Notes: Manufacturer receiver-control suite not in the list

Name: ARSVCOM
Function: Control Yaesu/Kenpro rotators via an ARS interface with satellite/moon tracking
Radio Interaction: serial rotator control
Notes: EB4EQA; EA4TX ARS ecosystem

Name: CalicoCAT
Function: Serial CAT-control plugin for SDR# (SDRSharp) enabling it to control an external transceiver.
Radio Interaction: Sends CAT frequency/mode commands over a serial/virtual port to the connected radio synced with SDR#'s tuning.
Notes: Adds transceiver rig-sync to the otherwise receive-only SDR# app.

Name: Cloud-SDR / CloudIQ software
Function: Remote-streaming client/server for RFSpace CloudIQ / Cloud-IQ
Radio Interaction: Controls the RFSpace networked SDR and streams IQ over the Internet
Notes: RFSpace's remote-SDR ecosystem.

Name: Commander (DXLab)
Function: Standalone CAT rig-control engine used by DXLab and 3rd-party software
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control (freq/mode/split), PTT keying, band data
Notes: Can drive the radio for many logging/digital apps.

Name: DDUtil
Function: Band-data decoder and CAT router for FlexRadio/PowerSDR stations, controlling amplifiers, tuners, antenna switches and providing shared CAT ports.
Radio Interaction: Reads the radio's CAT/band data (PowerSDR CAT or serial) and re-shares it to multiple apps via virtual serial ports/OmniRig.
Notes: By Steve Nance K5FR; the classic Flex accessory-automation hub.

Name: DXLab Commander
Function: Free CAT controller for up to 4 Icom, Ten-Tec, Kenwood, Yaesu, Elecraft, FlexRadio, Alinco or Kachina transceivers; member of the DXLab Suite.
Radio Interaction: CAT/CI-V serial control of frequency/mode/split/PTT; SpotCollector and other DXLab apps tune the radio through Commander.
Notes: By Dave Bernstein AA6YQ; acts as the CAT hub for the DXLab ecosystem.

Name: EA4TX ARSWIN / ARS-USB software
Function: Control antenna rotators (Yaesu/Kenpro/Daiwa/Prosistel/HAM-IV/T2X) via the ARS-USB interface
Radio Interaction: serial/USB rotator control; DDE to loggers
Notes: Rotator control in the same scope as the listed PstRotator/rotctld

Name: flrig
Function: Graphical rig-control front end (part of the fldigi suite) that displays and sets all radio parameters and acts as a CAT server/broker for other programs.
Radio Interaction: Connects to the radio's CAT port; exposes an XML-RPC server (port 12345) and Hamlib-style access so fldigi, WSJT-X and others share control; keys PTT.
Notes: By Dave Freese W1HKJ; widely used as the CAT broker behind fldigi and as a standalone panel.

Name: FRStack
Function: FlexRadio automation/station-control utility exposing rig control, band/antenna macros and a REST/HTTP interface for the Flex 6000/8000.
Radio Interaction: Uses the SmartSDR TCP API to read/set slice frequency, mode, TX and keying, and to automate accessories.
Notes: Popular Flex 'glue' tool for external automation and web control.

Name: FT-817 Commander
Function: Legacy dedicated CAT control panel for the Yaesu FT-817/857/897 family.
Radio Interaction: CAT serial control of frequency/mode/PTT on FT-8x7 radios.
Notes: Early HB9DRV-era utility still used by FT-817 owners.

Name: Green Heron Everyware
Function: Control Green Heron RT-21/RT-20 rotator controllers over network or serial
Radio Interaction: rotator control (serial/UDP)

Name: Grig
Function: Simple graphical Hamlib front end providing a virtual radio front panel.
Radio Interaction: Uses Hamlib to set/read frequency, mode and PTT over CAT.
Notes: By Alexandru Csete OZ9AEC; lightweight demonstrator of Hamlib rig control.

Name: Ham Radio Deluxe (HRD Rig Control + Remote)
Function: Suite whose Rig Control module offers deep CAT control of Icom/Kenwood/Yaesu/Elecraft/Ten-Tec radios, with a remote-operation server/client.
Radio Interaction: CAT/CI-V serial control aiming to expose every programmable command; HRD Remote streams control (and audio via companion tools) over IP.
Notes: Originally by Simon Brown HB9DRV; now a commercial product; DM780 handles digital modes.

Name: HamClock
Platforms: Linux / Raspberry Pi / macOS / Windows / Web
Function: Ham operating dashboard (clocks, propagation, DX) that can display and set the connected rig's frequency and mode
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control via Hamlib rigctld (reads/sets frequency and mode)
Notes: By Elwood Downey WB0OEW; the rig-link feature is what qualifies it beyond a pure reference tool.

Name: Hamlib (rigctl / rigctld / rigctlcom)
Platforms: Windows / macOS / Linux / Raspberry Pi / Android (library)
Function: Universal rig/rotator control library exposing a common API and a network daemon (rigctld) that virtually every Linux/open-source ham app uses to control a radio; supports 200+ transceivers.
Radio Interaction: CAT/CI-V serial and network control of frequency/mode/PTT; rigctld TCP server (port 4532) drives radios for WSJT-X, fldigi, CQRLOG, Gpredict, etc.; rigctlcom emulates a Kenwood radio to bridge apps.
Notes: The de facto CAT backbone of open-source ham software; rotctl/rotctld sibling handles rotators.

Name: Hamlib (rigctl / rigctld)
Platforms: Linux / Windows / macOS / Raspberry Pi / Android (Termux)
Function: Common CAT rig-control library, CLI and network daemon supporting hundreds of transceiver models
Radio Interaction: CAT/CI-V rig control (frequency/mode/PTT) over serial/USB/network; exposes rigctld TCP 4532 for other apps
Notes: The control backbone under Pat, HamClock, WSJT-X, Fldigi and many others; runs on Android via Termux.

Name: Hamlib / rigctld
Function: Universal rig-control library and network daemon
Radio Interaction: Provides CAT/PTT control of hundreds of transceivers; the tuning backend used by Gqrx, SDRangel, SDR++, wfview and Gpredict Doppler control
Notes: The cross-platform equivalent role to OmniRig; nearly every Linux SDR app links it.

Name: IC7100BKT / CatBkt (BktSoftware)
Function: Rig-control programs (IC7100BKT for the Icom IC-7100; CatBkt as a general CAT controller) with sked management, cluster tuning, PTT and tuner control and audio recording.
Radio Interaction: CI-V/CAT serial control of frequency/mode/PTT and tuner; direct-frequency input from cluster programs.
Notes: By Maurizio Vinci IZ2BKT (BktSoftware / maniaradio.it).

Name: Icom RS-BA1
Function: Icom IP remote-control software for compatible transceivers
Radio Interaction: CI-V rig control plus audio over IP and PTT
Notes: Icom's factory remote package.

Name: Icom RS-BA1 (Version 2)
Function: Icom's IP remote-control software providing a full radio front panel with spectrum scope over LAN/Internet.
Radio Interaction: CI-V control plus digital audio over IP to compatible Icom transceivers (with RS-BA1 server/Remote Utility).
Notes: Works with IC-7300/7610/9700/705 and older rigs via CI-V interface.

Name: Icom RS-R8600
Function: Remote-control software for the IC-R8600 wideband communications receiver
Radio Interaction: USB/LAN CI-V remote control + spectrum scope
Notes: Icom; CS-R8600 is the separate cloning/programming tool

Name: jAReC
Function: Java 'Amateur Radio Control' app that acts as both a remote-control server and client for CAT-enabled transceivers, with audio.
Radio Interaction: Connects to the rig via serial/USB/Bluetooth/network for CAT + bidirectional audio; serves control to remote clients like PocketRxTx.
Notes: Companion server to PocketRxTx by YO3GGX; cross-platform via Java.

Name: K3NG Rotator Controller
Function: Open-source Arduino azimuth/elevation rotator controller speaking Yaesu GS-232 and EasyComm protocols
Radio Interaction: serial rotator protocol
Notes: Anthony Good K3NG; rotator analog of his listed K3NG CW keyer

Name: K4-Control
Function: Comprehensive local and remote-over-internet control of the Elecraft K4 transceiver
Radio Interaction: Network/serial CAT control of the K4 (frequency, mode, panadapter, functions)
Notes: By Roskosch; dedicated Elecraft K4 front-panel-on-a-phone app.

Name: Kenwood ARCP / ARHP / ARVP suite
Function: Kenwood's free Amateur Radio Control Program (ARCP-990/590/2000), Host Program (ARHP) and VoIP Program (ARVP) for local and networked control.
Radio Interaction: CAT control of Kenwood transceivers; ARHP hosts the rig on a network and ARVP carries the audio for remote operation.
Notes: Manufacturer software for TS-590/TS-990/TS-2000 and similar.

Name: Kenwood ARCP / ARHP series
Function: Kenwood factory radio-control panels (ARCP-590/990/2000) and host programs (ARHP)
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control (freq/mode/menus), PTT; ARHP enables LAN remote
Notes: Free downloads from Kenwood for supported rigs.

Name: KX3 Companion
Function: Android remote control and logging for the Elecraft KX3
Radio Interaction: CAT over serial/Bluetooth
Notes: Phone-based control app for an Elecraft radio; complements the listed KX3/K3 Utility

Name: LP-Bridge
Function: CAT-sharing bridge for the Elecraft K3 that multiplexes one radio port to several applications (logger, panadapter, skimmer).
Radio Interaction: Talks to the K3 CAT port and re-exposes multiple virtual K3 ports, keeping frequency/mode in sync across apps.
Notes: By Larry Phipps N8LP (LP-PAN maker); commonly paired with NaP3 + CW Skimmer.

Name: microHAM USB Device Router
Function: Configuration and routing software for microHAM interfaces (microKEYER, DIGI KEYER, u2R, etc.) that manages CAT, PTT, CW/FSK keying and virtual ports.
Radio Interaction: Routes CAT to/from the radio, keys PTT and CW/FSK, and creates virtual COM ports so logging/digital apps reach the rig through microHAM hardware.
Notes: Essential glue for microHAM interface owners.

Name: N4PY
Function: Rig control software for Ten-Tec, Elecraft, Icom and other radios
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control, with virtual ports to share the rig with logging/digital software
Notes: By N4PY (Carl Moreschi).

Name: N4PY Rig Control
Function: Feature-rich rig control for Elecraft K3/K4, Icom, Ten-Tec, Flex and many receivers, integrating panadapters, WinKeyer, TMATE2/FlexControl knobs, amps and autotuners.
Radio Interaction: CAT/CI-V serial control; drives external panadapter programs and hardware controllers alongside the radio.
Notes: By Carl Moreschi N4PY; noted for deep hardware integration (TMATE2, FlexControl, Behringer).

Name: NaP3
Function: Panadapter/waterfall display software (PowerSDR-derived) for the Elecraft K3 and IF/IQ SDRs like LP-PAN and SDRplay.
Radio Interaction: Displays radio IF/IQ spectrum and controls the K3 VFOs, filters, bands and modes via CAT while clicking on the panadapter.
Notes: Classic K3 + LP-PAN panadapter; often chained with LP-Bridge and CW Skimmer.

Name: OmniRig
Function: CAT rig-control COM engine shared by many SDR apps
Radio Interaction: Provides CAT tuning/PTT control of a conventional transceiver to SDR#, HDSDR, SDRuno, NaP3, etc. so the SDR panadapter and rig stay in sync
Notes: Alex Shovkoplyas VE3NEA; the standard CAT bridge on Windows SDR panadapters.

Name: OmniRig (v1 & v2)
Function: Shared COM-object CAT engine that lets multiple Windows apps control the same transceiver simultaneously through one serial connection.
Radio Interaction: CAT/CI-V serial control of frequency/mode/PTT; acts as a broker so HDSDR, SDR Console, logging and digital apps share the rig's COM port.
Notes: By Alex Shovkoplyas VE3NEA; OmniRig v2 adds more rigs and features; ini-file rig definitions are user-editable.

Name: PocketRxTx
Function: Multimode Android transceiver-control app with waterfall/spectrum, memories, and WebSDR client mode.
Radio Interaction: Direct CAT/CI-V over Bluetooth, USB-OTG or network to Yaesu/Kenwood/Icom/Elecraft rigs; controls mode/band/VFO/PTT/ATU and can use a jAReC server for audio.
Notes: By Dan Toma YO3GGX; a leading Android CAT app.

Name: RCForb Client (RemoteHams)
Function: Free client for the RemoteHams network that operates shared/remote stations with rig control, audio and a bandmap.
Radio Interaction: Sends CAT commands and streams TX/RX audio and PTT to a remote radio via the RCForb Server over the Internet.
Notes: Part of the RemoteHams.com community remote-station platform.

Name: RCForb Server (RemoteHams)
Function: Server that publishes a local station to the RemoteHams network for remote operation.
Radio Interaction: Connects to the radio's CAT port and sound card, keys PTT, and relays control/audio to RCForb clients.
Notes: The station-host half of RemoteHams; supports many rigs via built-in CAT and Hamlib/OmniRig.

Name: Remote Ham Radio (RHR)
Function: Commercial shared-station service giving members web-console access to big remote stations with waterfall panadapter, filters and audio.
Radio Interaction: Browser/app console sends CAT tuning and PTT and streams audio to professionally hosted remote transceivers.
Notes: remotehamradio.com; access to high-power multi-antenna sites.

Name: RemoteHams RCForb
Function: Remote-radio network client/server for sharing/operating rigs online
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control plus audio over IP and PTT to a networked transceiver
Notes: RCForb Server shares a rig; RCForb Client operates it remotely.

Name: RemoteRig / RRC-Nano
Function: Low-latency remote-control system (RRC-1258 boxes) with the RRC-Nano app acting as a software radio-control unit for the remote base.
Radio Interaction: Transports serial CAT, PTT and audio between control and radio ends; RRC-Nano provides CAT control and audio to the remote rig over IP.
Notes: By Microbit; popular for Elecraft K3/KX3 'twin' remote setups.

Name: RemoteTX
Function: Subscription remote-control platform for Icom/Yaesu radios using a Raspberry Pi at the station and browser/app clients.
Radio Interaction: Pi connects to the rig's CAT/USB and audio; clients tune, key PTT and pass audio over the Internet.
Notes: Supports IC-7100/7200/7300/7610, FT-991A and others.

Name: rig-remote
Function: Lightweight Hamlib rigctld GUI for frequency scanning, bookmarks and control
Radio Interaction: TCP to rigctld (CAT)

Name: RigPi Station Server
Function: Raspberry Pi web-server appliance (MFJ) for remote operation of almost any radio from a browser, including CW keyer and logging.
Radio Interaction: Uses Hamlib to control the rig's CAT port, keys PTT/CW, and streams audio for remote web clients.
Notes: By Howard Nurse W6HN; browser-based control from phone/tablet/PC.

Name: SDR-Control Mobile
Function: iPhone edition giving complete local/remote operation of Icom IC-705/7300Mk2/7610/9700/7760 with waterfall, FT8/FT4, CW/SSTV and logging.
Radio Interaction: CI-V control plus audio/PTT over USB/WLAN or Internet to the Icom transceiver, no extra hardware needed.
Notes: By Marcus Roskosch DL3MRE; actively updated (2026).

Name: SmartSDR for iOS
Function: Third-party iOS/iPad client for FlexRadio 6000/8000 SDR transceivers with panadapter and full control.
Radio Interaction: Uses the Flex SmartSDR TCP API and SmartLink to control slices, tune, key PTT and stream audio, locally or remotely.
Notes: By Marcus Roskosch DL3MRE; the popular mobile Flex client.

Name: SoapySDR / SoapyRemote
Function: Vendor-neutral SDR hardware abstraction layer plus a network remoting server
Radio Interaction: Presents a common API to tune/control virtually any SDR; SoapyRemote controls a device on another host over the network
Notes: The plumbing under CubicSDR, SDRangel, SDR++ and many others.

Name: TMATE2 controller software
Function: Driver/integration for the Woodbox Radio TMATE and TMATE2 USB tuning-knob controllers (3 knobs, keys, display) used to control radios and panadapter software.
Radio Interaction: USB knob sends tuning/mode/PTT commands that host software (N4PY, NaP3, PowerSDR) translates into CAT control of the rig.
Notes: Hardware tuning knob; software support is built into N4PY, NaP3 and others.

Name: TRX-Manager
Function: CAT control, logging and DX program controlling ~150 transceivers plus rotators, wattmeters and antennas locally or remotely.
Radio Interaction: CAT/CI-V serial control of the rig; can operate rigs remotely and share control with other software.
Notes: By Laurent Labourie F6DEX; long-established all-in-one station controller.

Name: TS-Control
Function: Remote and local control of Kenwood TS-890 / TS-990 transceivers from a phone or tablet
Radio Interaction: Network/serial CAT control of the Kenwood rig
Notes: By Roskosch; Kenwood counterpart to his Icom/Flex/Elecraft control apps.

Name: W4MQ Internet Remote Base
Function: Legacy Internet remote-base software (server + client) that pioneered web-connected remote HF operation.
Radio Interaction: Server controls the rig's CAT port and audio; clients send tuning/PTT and receive audio over IP.
Notes: By Stan Schretter W4MQ; conceptual predecessor to modern remote platforms.

Name: Waterfall Bandmap
Function: Panadapter/bandmap that shows spectrum from any IQ SDR or IF output and overlays DX-cluster spots, working with almost any transceiver.
Radio Interaction: Reads rig frequency via CAT and tunes the radio by clicking spots on the waterfall; uses SDR/IF I/Q for the display.
Notes: By David AF9GL; turns any radio into a click-to-tune panadapter.

Name: wfview
Function: Open-source control app for modern Icom (plus Kenwood/Yaesu) radios with full spectrum scope/waterfall and network remote to another wfview instance.
Radio Interaction: CI-V/CAT control locally or over LAN/Internet; streams audio; can emulate a Hamlib rigctld server so other apps reach the rig.
Notes: By Elliott Liggett W6EL & Roeland Jansen PA3RVL; popular free alternative to RS-BA1.

Name: Win4Icom / Win4Yaesu / Win4K3-K4 Suite
Function: Full rig control with panadapter/SDR integration and virtual COM ports to feed loggers
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control (CI-V for Icom, CAT for Yaesu/Elecraft), PTT, band data; provides virtual radios to N1MM+/HRD/DXLab/Log4OM
Notes: By VA2FSQ (Tom Blahovici).

Name: Win4K3Suite / Win4IcomSuite / Win4YaesuSuite
Function: Commercial control suites for Elecraft (K4/K3/K3S/KX3/KX2), Icom and Yaesu radios with built-in panadapter (LP-PAN/SDRplay) and an HRD-compatible server.
Radio Interaction: CAT/CI-V control of the rig, integrated SDR panadapter display, and a server interface so loggers/digital apps read frequency/band/mode.
Notes: By Tom Blahovici VA2FSQ; three-radio-family editions with SDR panadapter support.

Name: Yaesu SCU-LAN10
Function: Yaesu network remote-control software for FTDX/FT-DX radios
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control plus audio over LAN and PTT via the SCU-LAN10 interface
Notes: Requires the SCU-LAN10 hardware bridge.

Name: Yaesu SCU-LAN10 Network Remote
Function: Yaesu remote-control application paired with the SCU-LAN10 interface for Ethernet/Internet operation of recent Yaesu rigs.
Radio Interaction: Sends CAT commands and streams audio/PTT to FTDX101/FTDX10/FT-710 through the SCU-LAN10 hardware.
Notes: Manufacturer network remote solution for Yaesu HF radios.

Name: AMSATDroid Free
Function: G4DPZ pass predictor showing next passes, compass/map views, and Doppler-corrected frequencies.
Radio Interaction: No direct CAT; computes Doppler-corrected RX/TX frequencies for manual tuning.
Notes: Long-standing AMSAT Android staple; recently open-sourced. Borderline (prediction-only) but a canonical sat app.

Name: CALSAT32 (Calsat)
Function: JR1HUO satellite tracking program with automatic Doppler and antenna control.
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control (Icom CI-V and others) for Doppler tuning plus rotator control.
Notes: Widely used in Japan for LEO sat operation.

Name: CardSat
Function: Self-contained handheld satellite tracker firmware with pass prediction.
Radio Interaction: CAT Doppler control to a connected radio.
Notes: Portable hardware tracker that keys a rig via CAT.

Name: FoxTelem
Function: AMSAT telemetry analysis tool decoding Fox-1, HuskySat, GOLF and similar FEC/DUV/BPSK downlinks.
Radio Interaction: Takes sound-card audio from a receiver; newer builds can command an SDR/Hamlib rig for signal-finding and Doppler.
Notes: Primarily an audio telemetry decoder that uploads to AMSAT.

Name: gp2icom
Function: Bridge utility that relays Gpredict Doppler frequencies to an Icom radio.
Radio Interaction: Sends CI-V CAT commands to set the Icom RX/TX frequency for Doppler.
Notes: Helper for Icom rigs lacking direct Hamlib satellite support.

Name: Gpredict
Function: Real-time multi-satellite tracking and orbit prediction with automatic Doppler tuning and rotor pointing.
Radio Interaction: Talks to Hamlib rigctld over TCP to set radio frequency for Doppler (and rotctld for rotors); tunes real rigs and SDR receivers.
Notes: The standard free/Linux choice; forks like gpredict-improved add built-in Hamlib Doppler and SDR/HackRF support.

Name: gr-satellites
Function: EA4GPZ GNU Radio out-of-tree module decoding telemetry from a large catalog of cubesats/amateur sats.
Radio Interaction: Ingests SDR I-Q with Doppler correction blocks; drives the SDR receive chain.
Notes: Reference decoder framework for many amateur satellites.

Name: GridTracker (GridTrackerSat)
Function: Mapping/logging companion to WSJT-X that adds a satellite tracking/pass mode and station automation.
Radio Interaction: Hamlib CAT rig control (frequency/mode); works alongside WSJT-X audio; satellite Doppler/pass overlay.
Notes: Popular WSJT-X sidecar; satellite features branded GridTrackerSat.

Name: Ham Radio Deluxe Satellite Tracking
Function: Satellite tracker within the HRD suite with automatic Doppler-shift calculation for transponders.
Radio Interaction: Uses HRD Rig Control (CAT over serial/USB) to retune the radio for Doppler.
Notes: Integrated with HRD logging and rig control.

Name: Ham Satting
Function: Multi-platform all-in-one satellite app with real-time visualization, pass prediction, and QSO logging.
Radio Interaction: Rig control / Doppler tuning via CAT or network (rigctld) integration.
Notes: Cross-platform, including mobile.

Name: Hamlib (rigctld / rotctld)
Function: Library and network daemons that provide the rig- and rotor-control layer used by satellite/EME trackers to apply Doppler.
Radio Interaction: Directly sets radio frequency/mode over serial/USB/CI-V and keys PTT; controls rotators — the conduit Gpredict/SatDump/SatNOGS use.
Notes: Not a tracker itself but the actual Doppler CAT mechanism for most Linux/free tools.

Name: HamSat
Function: iPhone/iPad satellite tracker with a full-screen in-pass display showing a Doppler-corrected RX/TX dial.
Radio Interaction: CAT control of a radio (e.g., Bluetooth to IC-705 or network rigctld) for Doppler tuning.
Notes: iOS in-pass operating aid.

Name: InstantTrack
Function: Classic AMSAT real-time satellite tracker and predictor.
Radio Interaction: Rig control and rotor control via external interfaces (Kansas City Tracker, PacComm, etc.) for Doppler and pointing.
Notes: Historic standard still run under DOSBox by some operators.

Name: iridium-toolkit / gr-iridium
Function: Capture and decode Iridium satellite frames and messages
Radio Interaction: SDR (Airspy/HackRF/USRP) at L-band
Notes: Utility satellite monitoring, same scope as the listed SatDump/dump1090

Name: KlaTrack
Function: Cross-platform satellite tracker with elevation/time plots and station control.
Radio Interaction: Rig CAT control and rotator control during passes.
Notes: Cross-platform alternative tracker.

Name: Look4Sat
Function: Open-source amateur/weather satellite tracker and pass predictor that displays Doppler-corrected frequencies.
Radio Interaction: Network rig control (rigctld / Gpredict-compatible TCP) to Doppler-tune a rig, plus rotator control over TCP.
Notes: Uses Celestrak TLEs and the SatNOGS transmitter database; the notable Doppler-aware Android app.

Name: MacDoppler
Function: Satellite tracking with any level of automation from assisted Doppler tuning and antenna pointing up to a fully automated sat gateway.
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control (serial/USB/network) for Doppler RX/TX and PTT, plus rotator control.
Notes: Premier Mac satellite app; successor to MacDopplerPRO.

Name: MacDopplerPRO
Function: Older PRO-generation Mac satellite tracker with Doppler tuning and antenna pointing.
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control + rotator control.
Notes: Superseded by the current MacDoppler; still referenced on older installs.

Name: MoonSked
Function: GM4JJJ EME scheduling and moon-tracking program with Doppler/degradation info and sked planning.
Radio Interaction: Rotator control to point at the Moon; can drive supported rigs and shows Doppler for manual/auto tuning.
Notes: Classic moonbounce planning tool.

Name: Nova for Windows
Function: Satellite tracking and pass prediction with rig and rotator control.
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control and rotor control (also usable as a DDE tracking source for SDR software).
Notes: Long-standing classic tracker.

Name: Orbitron
Function: Popular free real-time satellite tracker and pass predictor that feeds Doppler-corrected frequencies to radios and SDR apps.
Radio Interaction: Exports RX/TX frequency via DDE (MyDDE / WispDDE driver) to a CAT rig-control driver or to SDR#/SDRuno for Doppler correction.
Notes: Needs an external DDE driver/plugin to actually key CAT; ubiquitous as the tracking front-end for SDR Doppler.

Name: OscarWatch-Tracker
Function: AMSAT-oriented tracker that predicts passes and computes Doppler-corrected uplink/downlink frequencies.
Radio Interaction: Drives the rotator and CAT-controls the radio for Doppler during a pass.
Notes: Cross-platform spacecraft tracker.

Name: PREDICT
Function: KD2BD classic text/curses satellite tracker and pass predictor with a network server mode.
Radio Interaction: EasyComm rotor control and a UDP/socket Doppler server that feeds rig-control clients/scripts.
Notes: Long-lived Linux tracker; companion utilities do the actual CAT tuning.

Name: PstRotator
Function: Antenna rotator controller with a satellite mode (Doppler on RX/TX) and a dedicated EME/moon-tracking window.
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control for Doppler tuning (reads the SatPC32 Doppler.SQF file) and rotor control; EME moon azimuth/elevation and Doppler.
Notes: Bridges DDE from trackers; widely used for both LEO sats and moonbounce.

Name: raspberry-noaa v2
Function: Automated NOAA/Meteor satellite capture, decode and scheduling on a Raspberry Pi
Radio Interaction: RTL-SDR + scheduled rtl_fm/SatDump/wxtoimg capture
Notes: Full station orchestration; distinct from SatDump/WXtoImg alone

Name: SatDump
Platforms: Windows, macOS, Linux, Android, Raspberry Pi
Function: Generic SDR satellite data processor/decoder (APT, LRPT, HRPT, LRIT/HRIT, telemetry) with a built-in tracker and scheduler.
Radio Interaction: Drives SDR hardware directly; optional Hamlib rigctld Doppler-corrected RX/TX tuning and rotctld rotator control during passes.
Notes: Modern all-in-one for weather/telemetry sats; auto-Doppler on the pass.

Name: SatPC32
Function: Real-time satellite tracking, pass prediction, and automatic Doppler tuning of TX/RX for SSB/CW/FM/digital birds; drives antenna rotators.
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control over serial/USB (sets RX & TX VFOs for Doppler, full-duplex), PTT, plus rotor control.
Notes: De-facto Windows standard for LEO sat work; its Doppler.SQF frequency file is reused by PstRotator and others.

Name: SatTrack
Function: Feature-rich satellite tracker with a future-pass planner that controls station hardware.
Radio Interaction: Controls radios (CAT) and antenna rotors during passes.
Notes: Modern Windows tracker (distinct from the old Unix SatTrack).

Name: SDR# (SDRSharp) + DDE Tracking / Gpredict plugin
Function: Popular SDR receiver made Doppler-aware via community plugins that follow a tracker.
Radio Interaction: Plugin retunes the SDR for Doppler from Orbitron (DDE) or Gpredict (network), correcting the receive frequency during a pass.
Notes: Common cheap RX-only satellite Doppler setup.

Name: SDR++ (SDRPlusPlus)
Function: Cross-platform general SDR receiver that can be Doppler-tuned by an external tracker.
Radio Interaction: Exposes a rigctl server so Gpredict/other trackers retune it for Doppler; controls its own SDR hardware.
Notes: Doppler correction achieved via external tracking software.

Name: SkyRoof
Function: VE3NEA open-source app combining satellite tracking with an SDR waterfall showing a Doppler-corrected frequency scale.
Radio Interaction: Controls an SDR receiver with Doppler-corrected tuning; CAT rig control and rotator support.
Notes: Newer integrated tracker+SDR aimed at LEO ham sats.

Name: UISS (Universal Interface for Soundcard Satellites)
Function: Packet and APRS terminal for satellites (ISS, PSAT, etc.)
Radio Interaction: Via AGWPE/soundmodem — sound-card audio in/out; PTT via serial/VOX
Notes: ON6MU. Popular for ISS packet/APRS digipeater work.

Name: WiSP / WiSP DDE
Function: AMSAT groundstation suite for tracking, Doppler, and PACSAT store-and-forward (digital) satellite operation.
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control + rotator via DDE, plus TNC/modem keying for packet/PACSAT birds.
Notes: Historic go-to for digital/store-and-forward satellites.

Name: WXtrack
Function: Predicts weather and ham satellite passes and drives capture stations
Radio Interaction: pass scheduling to receivers/decoders; rotor/rig via companions
Notes: David Taylor GM8ARV (SatSignal.eu suite)

Name: Zenith
Function: Modern web-based satellite tracker for amateur operators with real-time tracking, pass prediction, and radio integration.
Radio Interaction: Browser/host-side rig integration (Web Serial / rigctld) for Doppler-aware tuning.
Notes: Self-hostable browser app.

Name: AbracaDABra
Function: DAB/DAB+ SDR receiver with a clean UI
Radio Interaction: Controls RTL-SDR/Airspy and SoapySDR devices
Notes: Petr Kopecký; modern Qt DAB receiver.

Name: Afedri SDR software / SDR-Net
Function: Control/receiver software for Afedri SDR-Net / AFE822x
Radio Interaction: Controls Afedri hardware over USB/Ethernet; ExtIO module lets HDSDR/SDR# use it
Notes: Afedri hardware also works with CuteSDR, Linrad, SdrDx, Zeus, etc.

Name: CubicSDR
Function: Cross-platform SDR receiver with multiple demodulators on a wideband waterfall
Radio Interaction: Controls any SoapySDR-supported device (RTL, Airspy, HackRF, LimeSDR, SDRplay, etc.) — tune/gain/demod
Notes: Charles Cliffe; SoapySDR-based so it inherits broad hardware support.

Name: cuSDR
Function: Early openHPSDR console
Radio Interaction: Controls HPSDR hardware over Ethernet
Notes: Hermann DL3HVH; largely historical now.

Name: CuteSDR
Function: Reference networked-SDR receiver
Radio Interaction: Controls RFSpace SDR-IP / NetSDR over Ethernet (tune, sample-rate)
Notes: Moe Wheatley AE4JY; codebase reused in many later SDR apps.

Name: DATV-Express
Function: Control/streaming software for DVB-S/S2 digital ATV transmission
Radio Interaction: drives DATV-Express exciter board or SDR (Pluto/LimeSDR) TX
Notes: Sets freq/symbol-rate/FEC; 70 MHz-3.4 GHz

Name: Dream
Function: Digital Radio Mondiale receiver/analyzer
Radio Interaction: Soundcard IQ / ExtIO SDR front-end for the DRM signal
Notes: The classic open DRM decoder; often paired with an SDR feeding baseband.

Name: DSDPlus (DSD+)
Function: Digital voice decoder (DMR, P25, NXDN, ProVoice, etc.)
Radio Interaction: With the DSD+ FastLane tools, controls RTL-SDR hardware (via librtlsdr) to tune RF; otherwise takes discriminator/virtual-audio from an SDR app
Notes: FastLane subscription includes the RF-tuning helper apps (FMPn/FMP).

Name: DSP Radio
Function: Simple Mac App Store SDR receiver
Radio Interaction: Controls RTL-SDR and soundcard front-ends for AM/FM/SSB/CW
Notes: An easy native macOS entry point to RTL-SDR.

Name: dump1090 / readsb
Function: Mode-S / ADS-B (1090 MHz) decoder with web map
Radio Interaction: Controls an RTL-SDR (and others) — tunes 1090 MHz and demodulates aircraft transponders
Notes: Basis of FlightAware PiAware, tar1090, etc.

Name: dump978
Function: 978 MHz UAT decoder for the US ADS-B UAT link
Radio Interaction: RTL-SDR
Notes: FlightAware companion to dump1090 (which is listed)

Name: Elad FDM-SW2
Function: Control/receiver software for Elad FDM-DUO SDR transceiver and FDM-S receivers with panadapter and wideband recording.
Radio Interaction: Controls the Elad SDR hardware for RX (and DUO TX/CAT) with I/Q spectrum display; provides CAT to external software.
Notes: Pairs with the FDM-DUO standalone/SDR hybrid transceiver.

Name: ExpertSDR2
Function: Expert Electronics console for SunSDR2/2 Pro/DX, ColibriNANO and MB1
Radio Interaction: Controls SunSDR hardware over Ethernet/USB — full TX/RX, CAT, remote
Notes: Ships with SunSDR/ColibriNANO products.

Name: ExpertSDR2 / ExpertSDR3
Function: Native software for Expert Electronics SunSDR2/DUO/MB1 SDR transceivers with wideband panadapter, multi-RX and remote operation.
Radio Interaction: Controls the SunSDR hardware over Ethernet for RX/TX/PTT; exposes CAT and virtual audio and supports E-Coder/network remote.
Notes: ExpertSDR3 is the current generation; ExpertRemote enables Internet operation.

Name: ExpertSDR3
Function: Newer Expert Electronics SDR suite for SunSDR/MB1 with client-server remote
Radio Interaction: Controls SunSDR hardware — TX/RX, panadapter, network remote
Notes: Successor generation to ExpertSDR2.

Name: G8JCFSDR
Function: General SDR receiver
Radio Interaction: Controls SoftRock/SDR-IQ/soundcard front-ends with CAT tuning
Notes: Peter Carnegie G8JCF.

Name: ghpsdr3-alex / QtRadio (glSDR)
Function: Distributed HPSDR client-server (dspserver + QtRadio/glSDR clients)
Radio Interaction: dspserver controls HPSDR hardware; clients tune it over the network
Notes: Early networked HPSDR ecosystem; largely superseded but still referenced.

Name: GNU Radio / GNU Radio Companion
Function: Dataflow signal-processing toolkit for building custom SDR receivers/transmitters
Radio Interaction: Source/sink blocks (gr-osmosdr, UHD, SoapySDR) directly control SDR hardware for RX and TX
Notes: The engine under Gqrx, OP25 and countless projects.

Name: Gqrx
Function: Open-source SDR receiver with panadapter/waterfall and a built-in remote-control (rigctl-compatible) network interface.
Radio Interaction: Controls SDR receiver hardware; exposes an rigctld-style TCP server and can be driven by/drive Hamlib for frequency control.
Notes: By Alexandru Csete OZ9AEC; RX-focused but participates in CAT via Hamlib.

Name: gr-osmosdr (Osmocom source/sink)
Function: GNU Radio source/sink block giving unified access to many SDRs
Radio Interaction: Directly controls RTL/HackRF/BladeRF/Airspy/SoapySDR/USRP for RX/TX inside GNU Radio flowgraphs
Notes: The common hardware bridge for GNU Radio apps like Gqrx.

Name: HDSDR
Function: Popular SDR receiver/panadapter program with OmniRig integration for synchronizing to and tuning an external transceiver.
Radio Interaction: Processes SDR/IF I/Q for spectrum and uses OmniRig CAT to read/set the connected transceiver's frequency and mode.
Notes: Frequently used as a cheap panadapter for IF-tapped HF radios.

Name: ka9q-radio
Function: Fast-convolution multichannel SDR that receives many frequencies at once from one device
Radio Interaction: Controls RTL-SDR/Airspy R2/Airspy HF+/RX888 MkII/FunCube (and optionally SDRplay/BladeRF/HackRF) and multicasts demodulated channels over IP
Notes: Phil Karn KA9Q; excels at simultaneous WSPR/FT8 monitoring on modest hardware.

Name: KGKSDR (M0KGK SDR Decoder/Display)
Function: SDR receiver/decoder for SoftRock-class hardware
Radio Interaction: Soundcard IQ input with CAT tuning of the front-end
Notes: James (M0KGK); a legacy SoftRock-era receiver.

Name: KISS Konsole
Function: One of the first HPSDR consoles
Radio Interaction: Controls early HPSDR boards
Notes: Phil Covington N8VB; historical HPSDR software.

Name: KiwiSDR
Function: 0–30 MHz multi-user web SDR with built-in WSPR/FT8/FAX/NAVTEX/HFDL/TDoA decoders
Radio Interaction: Controls the Kiwi front-end hardware; each browser session tunes an independent channel
Notes: John Seamons ZL/KF6VO; the browser UI descends from the original OpenWebRX.

Name: Langstone
Function: Full SSB/CW/FM/DATV SDR transceiver for microwave bands
Radio Interaction: controls LimeSDR/PlutoSDR as a transceiver
Notes: G4EML

Name: linHPSDR
Function: Linux SDR console for HPSDR/ANAN transceivers with spectrum display and multi-receiver support.
Radio Interaction: Operates the SDR hardware for RX/TX and exposes control/audio interfaces.
Notes: Also by G0ORX; the desktop-Linux sibling of piHPSDR.

Name: Maia SDR
Function: Open FPGA SDR with real-time waterfall (up to 61.44 Msps) and a WebSDR-like browser UI
Radio Interaction: Runs on the Pluto and controls its AD936x front-end (RX; IQ recording)
Notes: Daniel Estévez EA4GPZ; promotes open FPGA+SDR development.

Name: MiniTioune
Function: Receive and decode DVB-S/S2 digital ATV, including QO-100 RB-TV, via a MiniTiouner tuner
Radio Interaction: USB control of MiniTiouner NIM tuner (it is the DATV receiver)
Notes: F6DZP; large ham DATV community; digital ATV absent from the list

Name: OpenHPSDR-PowerSDR (mRX PS)
Function: Community fork of PowerSDR for openHPSDR / ANAN radios
Radio Interaction: Controls HPSDR/ANAN over Ethernet (Protocol 1) with full RX/TX, PTT and CAT
Notes: Predecessor lineage to Thetis; still run on many ANAN stations.

Name: OpenWebRX+
Function: Community fork of OpenWebRX with additional decoders and UI features
Radio Interaction: Same hardware control as OpenWebRX (RTL/Airspy/SDRplay/HackRF)
Notes: Adds modes like more digital decoders and enhanced maps.

Name: Perseus software
Function: Microtelecom Perseus HF SDR receiver application
Radio Interaction: Controls the Perseus over USB — tuning, wideband IQ recording
Notes: Perseus is a benchmark HF SDR; the app is prized for spectrum recording.

Name: PhantomSDR / PhantomSDR-Plus
Function: Wideband web SDR backend able to sample the whole HF band
Radio Interaction: Controls RX888 MkII and other wideband SDRs; browser clients tune independent channels
Notes: Handles hundreds–thousands of concurrent users on capable hardware.

Name: piHPSDR
Function: Compact standalone SDR console for HPSDR/ANAN/Hermes-Lite radios with panadapter, designed for Raspberry Pi with touchscreen.
Radio Interaction: Drives the SDR hardware over Ethernet for RX/TX, PTT and CW; can act as a Hamlib server for other apps.
Notes: By John Melton G0ORX; popular embedded Flex/ANAN front panel.

Name: Portable SDR (PSDR)
Function: Self-contained handheld SDR transceiver + vector network analyzer
Radio Interaction: The onboard firmware controls the device's RX/TX front-end and keys transmit directly
Notes: Michael R. Colton's open PSDR project; a fully standalone SDR rather than a PC client.

Name: Portsdown (BATC)
Function: Raspberry-Pi digital ATV transmit/receive system covering common DATV modes
Radio Interaction: drives LimeSDR/Pluto TX + MiniTiouner/NIM RX
Notes: British Amateur TV Club Portsdown 4

Name: Pothos / PothosSDR (Pothos Flow)
Function: Dataflow SDR programming environment and Windows SoapySDR bundle
Radio Interaction: Controls SoapySDR-supported devices for RX/TX
Notes: The PothosSDR Windows installer is a common way to get SoapySDR/drivers on Windows.

Name: PowerSDR
Function: Original FlexRadio console for SDR-1000 / Flex-1500 / 3000 / 5000
Radio Interaction: Controls Flex hardware over USB/FireWire with full RX/TX and CAT emulation
Notes: The software that launched mainstream ham SDR; still used on legacy Flex radios.

Name: PowerSDR (mRX PS / OpenHPSDR)
Function: SDR transceiver console originally for FLEX-1500/3000/5000, now maintained for OpenHPSDR/ANAN radios, with panadapter and full control.
Radio Interaction: Operates the SDR hardware directly for RX/TX and provides CAT and virtual audio for external programs.
Notes: Legacy Flex and OpenHPSDR forks; ancestor of Thetis.

Name: Qt-DAB
Function: Full-featured DAB/DAB+ receiver
Radio Interaction: Controls RTL-SDR/SDRplay/Airspy/HackRF/LimeSDR front-ends
Notes: Jan van Katwijk; among the most complete open DAB decoders.

Name: Quisk
Function: Open-source SDR transceiver application for SoftRock, Hermes-Lite, HL2 and other radios with panadapter and full TX.
Radio Interaction: Operates the SDR hardware for RX/TX and PTT; provides Hamlib/CAT so digital apps can control it.
Notes: By Jim Ahlstrom N2ADR; highly configurable Python SDR.

Name: RF Analyzer
Function: Real-time FFT/waterfall spectrum analyzer and AM/FM/SSB/CW demodulator with IQ recording
Radio Interaction: Directly controls SDR receivers (RTL-SDR, HackRF, Airspy, HydraSDR) over USB-OTG
Notes: By Dennis Mantz; the most common Android SDR receiver app (Play Store + F-Droid).

Name: Rocky
Function: Lightweight SDR receiver/transmitter for SoftRock hardware
Radio Interaction: Soundcard IQ plus CAT/Si570 tuning of SoftRock kits; keys PTT for TX
Notes: Alex Shovkoplyas VE3NEA; classic SoftRock companion.

Name: rsp_tcp
Function: rtl_tcp-compatible network server for SDRplay RSP devices
Radio Interaction: Controls an SDRplay RSP and streams IQ to clients over the network
Notes: Lets rtl_tcp-only clients drive an RSP.

Name: rtl-sdr command-line tools (rtl_fm, rtl_tcp, rtl_power, rtl_sdr, rtl_test)
Function: Osmocom librtlsdr utilities for tuning, FM demod, IQ capture, power sweeps and network serving
Radio Interaction: Directly control RTL-SDR dongles — set frequency/gain/sample-rate, stream IQ or audio; rtl_tcp serves it on the network
Notes: The foundational toolset the entire RTL-SDR ecosystem builds on.

Name: RTL1090
Function: Mode-S/ADS-B decoder for RTL-SDR
Radio Interaction: RTL-SDR
Notes: jetvision; feeds Virtual Radar Server / PlanePlotter

Name: rtl_433
Function: Decoder for 433/315/868/915 MHz ISM devices (sensors, TPMS, weather stations)
Radio Interaction: Tunes and controls an RTL-SDR (and SoapySDR devices) to capture and demodulate ISM signals
Notes: Very widely used for home-automation/telemetry sniffing.

Name: rtl_tcp SDR (iOS)
Function: SDR receiver client for iPhone/iPad
Radio Interaction: Connects to a networked RTL-SDR via the rtl_tcp protocol (since iOS blocks direct USB SDRs) and tunes it remotely
Notes: Typical iOS pattern: a Pi/Mac runs rtl_tcp, the phone is the client.

Name: SatNOGS (satnogs-client / gr-satnogs)
Function: Open-source global networked satellite ground-station software that schedules and records passes automatically.
Radio Interaction: Hamlib rig control with Doppler correction, rotator control (rotctld), and SDR capture/decode.
Notes: Backbone of the SatNOGS observation network.

Name: SDR Console (SDR-Radio v3)
Function: Full-featured SDR receiver/transceiver console with panadapter, multi-VFO, and an 'External Radio' CAT link for hybrid operation.
Radio Interaction: Controls SDR hardware for RX/TX and can send/receive CAT (frequency/mode, Doppler) to an external transceiver via virtual serial ports.
Notes: By Simon Brown G4ELI; widely used for QO-100 with transverter/rig CAT sync.

Name: SDR Console (SDR-Radio.com)
Function: Multi-receiver RX and TX SDR suite with data recorder, DX cluster, satellite tracking and remote server
Radio Interaction: Controls a very broad range of RX and RX/TX SDRs plus CAT control of conventional transceivers; built-in networked remote-radio server
Notes: Simon Brown G4ELI; one of the most feature-complete Windows SDR packages.

Name: SDR Touch
Function: Portable SDR receiver/scanner and spectrum analyzer
Radio Interaction: Controls RTL-SDR/NeSDR/SDRplay/HackRF over USB OTG — tune and demod FM/AM/NFM/SSB
Notes: Martin Marinov; the classic phone/tablet SDR receiver.

Name: SDR# (SDRSharp)
Function: General-purpose SDR receiver with a large third-party plugin ecosystem (scanners, decoders, panadapter, scheduler)
Radio Interaction: Directly controls Airspy / RTL-SDR / HackRF / SDRplay and other front-ends (tune, gain, sample-rate); optional plugins add CAT/OmniRig control and PTT sync of an external transceiver
Notes: Airspy's flagship app; de-facto reference for the ExtIO-less .NET plugin world. Windows-only in current builds.

Name: SDR++
Platforms: Windows / macOS / Linux / Android / Raspberry Pi
Function: Modern, fast cross-platform SDR receiver with modular source/decoder plugins
Radio Interaction: Controls many SDRs via source modules; can drive an external rig over rigctl/network and act as a SpyServer/rtl_tcp client
Notes: Alexandre Rouma; frequently cited as the best general beginner/daily-driver receiver in 2026.

Name: SDR-J
Function: Jan van Katwijk's SDR suite (sdr-j-fm broadcast/RDS receiver, DAB tools)
Radio Interaction: Controls RTL-SDR/SDRplay/Airspy front-ends
Notes: The lineage that produced Qt-DAB.

Name: SDRangel
Platforms: Windows / macOS / Linux / Android / Raspberry Pi
Function: Multi-mode, multi-device SDR RX/TX application supporting many SDRs including transceiver-capable hardware, with spectrum/waterfall.
Radio Interaction: Directly operates SDR hardware for RX/TX and PTT; can also control external rigs and act as a network source.
Notes: Very broad hardware support; includes ham TX plugins.

Name: SDRconnect
Function: SDRplay's newer cross-platform receiver with integrated remote server/client
Radio Interaction: Controls SDRplay RSP devices locally or over the network
Notes: SDRplay's cross-platform answer to SDRuno.

Name: SdrDx
Function: Full-featured SDR receiver for networked and soundcard SDRs
Radio Interaction: Controls RFSpace SDR-IP/NetSDR, RTL-SDR and soundcard front-ends
Notes: Ben (fyngyrz); known for a dense, scriptable UI.

Name: SdrGlut
Function: SDR receiver derived from SdrDx, ported to SoapySDR
Radio Interaction: Controls any SoapySDR device (RTL, Airspy, HackRF, LimeSDR, etc.)
Notes: Ron Economou W6RZ.

Name: SDRTrunk
Function: Trunked and conventional radio monitor/recorder (P25, DMR, etc.)
Radio Interaction: Controls RTL-SDR/Airspy/HackRF/SDRplay tuners — follows control channels and tunes voice grants across the SDR's bandwidth
Notes: Dennis Sheirer; a leading open trunk-tracking application.

Name: SDRuno
Function: SDRplay's native receiver/panadapter application, also usable as an IF panadapter for transceivers with OmniRig CAT sync.
Radio Interaction: Controls SDRplay RSP hardware for RX and, via OmniRig, tracks/tunes an external transceiver's CAT frequency.
Notes: Common low-cost panadapter add-on for HF rigs with IF taps.

Name: SigDigger
Function: Real-time SDR digital signal analyzer and inspector
Radio Interaction: controls SDR hardware via SoapySDR
Notes: BatchDrake

Name: SliceMaster
Function: FlexRadio helper that auto-launches and positions CW Skimmer, N1MM, DAX and other apps per active slice/panadapter.
Radio Interaction: Monitors and drives Flex slices via the SmartSDR API, routing CAT/audio to the right instances of external software.
Notes: By Steve Hicks N4IRS; automates Flex + skimmer workflows.

Name: SmartSDR
Function: FlexRadio 6000/8000 Signature-series client with panadapter, DAX audio and CAT bridge
Radio Interaction: Controls Flex radios over Ethernet (local or SmartLink Internet) — TX/RX, slice receivers, DAX/CAT for digital-mode apps
Notes: Includes SmartSDR CAT and DAX for feeding WSJT-X/fldigi etc.

Name: SmartSDR (FlexRadio) + SmartSDR CAT / DAX / Maestro
Function: Native control software for FlexRadio 6000/8000 SDR transceivers with multiple spectrum/waterfall slices; SmartSDR CAT and DAX expose virtual CAT ports and audio to third-party apps.
Radio Interaction: Directly operates the Flex SDR over Ethernet (control + I/Q + audio); SmartSDR CAT provides serial CAT and DAX provides sound-card audio to loggers/digital software; SmartLink enables remote.
Notes: Maestro is the standalone hardware console running the same UI.

Name: SmartSDR for Mac / iOS (dogpark software)
Function: Third-party client for FlexRadio 6000-series
Radio Interaction: Controls the Flex over the network — TX/RX, slices, panadapter
Notes: Marcus Roskosch DL8MRE; the main native Apple client for Flex.

Name: SoDiRa
Function: DRM / AM / SSB software receiver
Radio Interaction: Controls SDR front-ends via ExtIO (RTL-SDR, etc.) and soundcard IQ
Notes: Focused on Digital Radio Mondiale broadcast decoding.

Name: SpectraVue
Function: RFSpace's official receiver/spectrum app
Radio Interaction: Controls RFSpace SDR-IQ / SDR-14 / NetSDR / CloudIQ over USB/Ethernet — tune, record, spectrum
Notes: Bundled with RFSpace hardware; also does data/IQ recording.

Name: Studio1
Function: Commercial general-purpose SDR receiver noted for low CPU use and advanced DSP
Radio Interaction: Controls SDR front-ends via ExtIO DLLs (RTL-SDR, Perseus, etc.)
Notes: SDR Applications / WoodBoxRadio; the codebase SDRuno grew from.

Name: Thetis
Function: Modern OpenHPSDR/Apache Labs ANAN SDR console (PowerSDR successor) with advanced panadapter, noise reduction and multi-RX.
Radio Interaction: Controls ANAN/Hermes-Lite SDR hardware for RX/TX; exposes CAT (TCP/serial) and virtual audio to third-party apps.
Notes: Maintained by the OpenHPSDR community for ANAN transceivers.

Name: trunk-recorder
Function: Records and logs P25/SmartNet/DMR trunked systems across control and voice channels
Radio Interaction: multiple SDRs (RTL/Airspy/HackRF/BladeRF) via GNU Radio/SoapySDR
Notes: Feeds OpenMHz/Broadcastify Calls; peer to the listed SDRTrunk/OP25

Name: Unitrunker
Function: Trunked-system decoder/controller that coordinates receivers
Radio Interaction: Commands SDRs (e.g. via SDR# link) or hardware scanners to follow trunked control/voice channels
Notes: Often paired with SDR# and DSD+ to build a trunk-tracking rig.

Name: Universal Radio Hacker (URH)
Function: Investigate, decode and replay/transmit wireless protocols with SDRs
Radio Interaction: drives SDRs (RTL/HackRF/USRP/LimeSDR/Airspy/SDRplay/PlutoSDR) TX and RX

Name: Wavesink Plus
Function: DAB+/FM broadcast SDR receiver
Radio Interaction: Controls RTL-SDR over USB OTG to receive DAB/DAB+ ensembles
Notes: One of the few polished mobile DAB decoders.

Name: WebSDR
Function: The original multi-user web SDR
Radio Interaction: Controls the SDR/soundcard front-end and streams tuned audio/waterfall to many simultaneous browser users
Notes: Pieter-Tjerk de Boer PA3FWM; powers hundreds of public receivers (websdr.org).

Name: welle.io
Platforms: Windows / Linux / macOS / Android / Raspberry Pi
Function: DAB/DAB+ software radio
Radio Interaction: Controls RTL-SDR/SDRplay/Airspy/SoapySDR devices over USB to receive DAB ensembles
Notes: Cross-platform including a good Android build.

Name: Winrad
Function: Original general-purpose SDR receiver by Alberto di Bene I2PHD
Radio Interaction: Soundcard IQ input and ExtIO hardware modules
Notes: Ancestor of HDSDR and Studio1; largely superseded but historically important.

Name: WiNRADiO software (Excalibur, G3x/G6x)
Function: Control/receiver software for WiNRADiO Excalibur (G31DDC/G33DDC/G65DDC) and G3 series SDRs
Radio Interaction: Controls WiNRADiO SDR receivers over USB — tuning, demod, recording
Notes: Ships with WiNRADiO hardware.

Name: Zeus Radio
Function: Console for the ZEUS ZS-1 SDR transceiver (RIT/XIT/split, NR, auto-notch)
Radio Interaction: Controls ZS-1 hardware TX/RX; also drives Hermes/ANAN/Afedri/Red Pitaya/HiQSDR/RTL-SDR front-ends
Notes: SSB Electronic / Xylo; ships with the ZS-1 transceiver.

Name: AGWPE (SV2AGW Packet Engine)
Function: Packet engine that exposes a soundcard TNC (or drives a hardware TNC) through the AGW API over TCP
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in/out (or controls a serial hardware TNC); PTT via serial port
License: Free; AGW Packet Engine Pro is commercial
Notes: SV2AGW. The AGW API it defined is used by dozens of APRS/packet apps; 'AGW mode' is a universal interface standard.

Name: AGWPE / AGW Packet Engine (Pro)
Function: Sound-card packet engine and host API (AGW protocol) serving multiple client apps simultaneously
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in/out; PTT via serial port; provides AX.25 packet to Winlink, APRS, BBS clients
Notes: By SV2AGW George Rossopoulos; the original sound-card TNC, now largely superseded by UZ7HO/Direwolf.

Name: ARDOP (ARDOP_Win / ARDOP TNC)
Function: Open sound-card modem implementing the Amateur Radio Digital Open Protocol (v1 and v2); free alternative modem for Winlink/Pat/ARIM
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in/out; PTT via CAT/serial/Hamlib; TCP or serial host interface to client apps
Notes: By Rick Muething KN6KB; the Windows ARDOP TNC ships with Winlink Express. Designed to replace WINMOR.

Name: ARDOP (ardopc / ardopcf / ARDOP_Win TNC)
Function: Open ARQ soundcard modem used by Winlink, Pat and ARIM
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in/out; CAT + PTT; presents a TCP virtual-TNC
Notes: KN6KB origin; ardopcf is the maintained cross-platform build.

Name: ARDOP_GUI
Function: Graphical monitor/front end for the ARDOP TNC showing waterfall, constellation, and status
Radio Interaction: Displays and controls the ARDOP TNC, which handles the sound-card audio and PTT
Notes: Diagnostic/tuning companion for ARDOP modems.

Name: ardopc / ardopcf
Function: C-language ARDOP TNC (ardopcf is the actively maintained fork); command-line, supports TCP or virtual-COM host
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio; PTT via Hamlib/rigctld/flrig, serial or GPIO; can emulate an SCS Dragon so PACTOR-only apps (Winlink Express, AirMail) can use ARDOP
Notes: github.com/... ardopcf. Core modem for headless/Linux Winlink stations.

Name: ARIM
Function: Amateur Radio Instant Messaging: keyboard messaging, file transfer, and a shared-file BBS over ARDOP
Radio Interaction: Connects to the ARDOP TNC, which drives sound-card audio and PTT on the rig
Notes: By Bob Cameron VE3TOK with ARDOP by KN6KB/G8BPQ. whitemesa.net/arim.

Name: ARIM / gARIM
Function: Amateur Radio Instant Messaging: keyboard chat, file and message transfer over ARDOP
Radio Interaction: Through an ARDOP soundcard modem (audio + PTT)
Notes: ncurses ARIM runs headless on a Pi; gARIM is the GTK GUI.

Name: BayCom / FlexNet (BayCom modem software)
Function: BayCom modem driver plus FlexNet L2/L3 packet networking stack
Radio Interaction: Baycom modem on serial/parallel (or soundcard); PTT via port lines
Notes: Legacy European packet standard; FlexNet32 provided the routing layer for GP/TSTHOST terminals.

Name: Dire Wolf
Function: Software 'soundcard' AX.25 packet modem/TNC and APRS encoder/decoder; also digipeater, IGate, APRStt gateway, tracker
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in/out (AFSK 1200/9600, 300 baud, FX.25/IL2P FEC); PTT via serial RTS/DTR, GPIO, CM108 HID, hamlib, or VOX
Notes: De-facto standard soft-TNC (WB2OSZ). Exposes AGW network protocol plus KISS serial and KISS-over-TCP; drives BPQ32, Xastir, YAAC, JNOS, Winlink, etc.

Name: gARIM
Function: GUI version of ARIM for ARDOP and ARDOP2 TNCs
Radio Interaction: Via the ARDOP TNC (sound-card audio + PTT)
Notes: Same feature set as ARIM with a graphical interface.

Name: piardopc / piardop2
Function: ARDOP v1 (piardopc) and v2 (piardop2) TNC builds for the Raspberry Pi
Radio Interaction: USB sound interface for audio; PTT via GPIO, serial or CAT/Hamlib
Notes: Standard modem for Pi-based Winlink/BPQ gateways.

Name: QtSoundModem
Function: GUI software TNC exposing KISS and AGW; multi-port soundcard AX.25 modem
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in/out; PTT via serial or GPIO
Notes: Cross-platform sibling of UZ7HO (port maintained by G8BPQ). Common on Pi packet nodes.

Name: soundmodem (Thomas Sailer)
Function: Kernel-integrated soundcard AX.25 modem (AFSK/FSK/PSK)
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in/out; PTT via serial/parallel port lines
Notes: Original *nix soundmodem; attaches to the Linux kernel AX.25 stack (distinct from UZ7HO SoundModem).

Name: TFPCX (The Firmware PC eXtended, DG0FT)
Function: Emulates a TNC in WA8DED host mode as a TSR for terminal programs and BayCom modems
Radio Interaction: Baycom/soundcard modem; PTT via serial line
Notes: Legacy driver that gave DOS terminals (GP, TSTHOST, TOP) a software TNC; TFKISS/TFPCR variants.

Name: UZ7HO SoundModem
Function: Dual-port software Packet-Radio TNC implementing AX.25 with many modems (1200, 9600, 300, Robust, QPSK, etc.)
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in/out; PTT via serial (RTS/DTR), CAT, or VOX
Notes: Two TCP interfaces: AGW PE (drop-in AGWPE replacement) and (X)KISS. Widely paired with BPQ32, EasyTerm, UISS.

Name: VARA FM / VARA HF
Function: High-speed OFDM soundcard modems used by Winlink and VarAC
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in/out; CAT + PTT via the host app
License: Commercial (free at reduced speed; paid unlock)
Notes: EA5HVK; the de-facto Winlink HF modem and a modern alternative to Pactor.

Name: VARA HF
Function: High-performance HF OFDM soft-modem (>7000 bps in 2.3 kHz) used as the modem for Winlink, VarAC, Pat, vARIM
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in/out over SSB; PTT via CAT/RTS-DTR/Hamlib/VOX. IP host interface to client apps
License: Donationware (free eval at reduced speed; paid lifetime license)
Notes: By EA5HVK (rosmodem). One license covers VARA HF + VARA FM. A software TNC, not hardware.

Name: VARA SAT
Function: VARA modem variant tuned for the QO-100 geostationary satellite
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio + PTT/CAT through a satellite-capable SSB station
Notes: Also by EA5HVK; for narrowband satellite data/email.

Name: VARA Terminal / VARA Chat
Function: Simple terminal and chat front ends bundled/associated with the VARA modem for point-to-point connects
Radio Interaction: Sends/receives through the VARA sound modem; PTT via VARA's rig-control settings
Notes: Lightweight alternatives to VarAC for testing/basic connects over VARA.

Name: vARIM
Function: ARIM-style instant messaging, file transfer and BBS built for the VARA HF modem
Radio Interaction: Drives the VARA HF sound modem (audio + PTT/CAT)
Notes: whitemesa.net/varim; VARA counterpart to ARIM/gARIM.

Name: WINMOR (WINMOR Sound Card TNC)
Function: Legacy HF sound-card modem (500 Hz & 1600 Hz) for Winlink; predecessor to ARDOP
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in/out; PTT via CAT/serial
Notes: By KN6KB. Deprecated in favor of ARDOP but still seen in older installs.

Name: WinRPR
Function: Soundcard emulation of the SCS Tracker TNC for HF/Robust Packet and normal HF packet
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in/out; PTT via serial or VOX; presents WA8DED host-mode and KISS over serial/TCP
Notes: G8BPQ. Revived Robust Packet without SCS hardware; used with BPQ32 (SCSTracker interface).

Name: Black Cat SSTV
Function: Send and receive SSTV images across common modes with tuning display and image library.
Radio Interaction: Sound-card / device-audio in/out to the radio; PTT via VOX or interface; on Mac can key via serial/CAT.
Notes: By Black Cat Systems. One of the few polished Apple-platform SSTV apps; App Store id387910013 for iOS.

Name: ChromaPIX
Function: Full-featured color SSTV program with visual template composer and DSP decoder.
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in/out; serial-port PTT keying.
Notes: By Silicon Pixels (Jim Barber N7CX). Classic Win9x/XP-era app, still run by enthusiasts.

Name: DigTRX
Function: DRM-based digital SSTV/data program for images and files over HF.
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in/out; serial-port PTT keying.
Notes: By PY4ZBZ/collaborators; an early HamDRM digital-SSTV/file tool related to the EasyPal lineage.

Name: DroidSSTV
Function: Send and receive SSTV images on Android with mode selection and image gallery.
Radio Interaction: Device audio to/from the radio (acoustic or cable); PTT via VOX or Wolphi interface cable.
Notes: By Wolphi. Pairs with the Wolphi-Link audio interface for a clean phone-to-rig connection.

Name: EasyPal
Function: Digital SSTV using DRM (COFDM) to send images and files (jpg/pdf/doc, ICS forms) in a 2.5 kHz channel with error correction.
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in/out; PTT via serial/CAT or VOX; CAT rig control supported.
Notes: By the late Erik Sundstrup VK4AES. The dominant HF digital-SSTV program; QSSTV is HAMDRM-compatible with it.

Name: Fldigi (WEFAX mode)
Function: Multimode digital modem app whose WEFAX-IOC576/288 modes decode and transmit HF weather fax with auto-align, phasing and auto-save.
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in/out; PTT via serial (RTS/DTR), Hamlib or flrig CAT; full CAT rig control.
Notes: By W1HKJ team. Free cross-platform first choice for WEFAX; also does dozens of non-image digital modes.

Name: GSHPC
Function: Sound-card SSTV and HF FAX program with RX/TX and image handling.
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in/out; serial-port PTT keying.
Notes: By Gerd Schrick / 'G. Schrick' Ham Picture package; long-standing free Windows option.

Name: Ham Radio Deluxe (DM780)
Function: HRD suite's Digital Master 780 module supports SSTV alongside PSK/RTTY/other digital modes, tightly integrated with HRD rig control.
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in/out; PTT and full CAT rig control via the HRD Rig Control engine.
Notes: SSTV is one of many modes; strongest as an integrated logging+rig-control+digital suite.

Name: HamFax
Function: Receive and transmit HF fax (WEFAX) and some SSTV via the sound card.
Radio Interaction: ALSA/OSS sound-card audio in/out; PTT keying via serial line (RTS/DTR) or Hamlib.
Notes: By Christof Schmitt DH1CS. Long-standing Linux/Unix fax program.

Name: HF Weather Fax
Function: Dedicated HF radiofax decoder tuned for marginal reception, with phasing/slant correction and automatic image capture.
Radio Interaction: Sound-card / device audio input from the receiver; receive-focused (no TX/PTT needed).
Notes: By Black Cat Systems. Polished cross-platform WEFAX app; iOS version lets a phone decode fax off a receiver.

Name: HOKA Code3-32 / W-CODE
Function: Professional-grade multimode decoders (HOKA Code3-32, Wavecom W-CODE) that include HF FAX and SSTV among hundreds of utility/maritime modes.
Radio Interaction: Sound-card or dedicated hardware audio input from the receiver; monitoring/receive-oriented.
Notes: High-end monitoring tools sometimes used by advanced amateurs for WEFAX/NAVTEX/SSTV.

Name: JNX
Function: Java-based decoder for NAVTEX (SITOR-B/FEC) and HF weather fax.
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio input; receive-only.
Notes: By IW0HDV. Cross-platform via Java; paired with YaND-style NAVTEX listening.

Name: JVComm32
Function: Multi-mode HF decoder for radiofax (WEFAX), SSTV, NAVTEX, RTTY and synoptic/SITOR modes; strong scheduler for automated fax reception.
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in/out; PTT/rig keying via COM port; supports CAT tuning to fax schedules.
Notes: By Eberhard Backeshoff DK8JV. A staple for maritime weather-fax listeners.

Name: JVFAX
Function: Classic DOS FAX/SSTV receive and transmit program supporting WEFAX and multiple SSTV modes.
Radio Interaction: Uses a simple comparator/soundcard interface for audio; serial-port PTT.
Notes: By DK8JV; JVFAX 7.x was ubiquitous in the 1990s and still used on retro setups.

Name: KG-STV
Function: Sound-card digital SSTV system sending standard-size images with error correction; lighter-weight than EasyPal/DRM, popular for HF nets.
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in/out; serial-port or VOX PTT.
Notes: By JJ0OBZ. Runs well under Wine on Linux; common on 20/40 m digital-SSTV nets.

Name: MMSSTV
Function: The de-facto standard free SSTV RX/TX program; supports Robot, Scottie, Martin, PD, Wraase and many other modes, with template/macro overlays, auto-slant correction, FFT tuning display and history.
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in/out for the SSTV tones; PTT keying via serial (RTS/DTR) or VOX; some CAT/rig helper add-ons for frequency.
Notes: By Makoto Mori JE3HHT. Development frozen but still the most widely used Windows SSTV app; basis for many others.

Name: MSCAN SSTV / MSCAN Meteo
Function: Color SSTV RX/TX with built-in DSP; the Meteo edition adds HF weather fax, NAVTEX and RTTY decoding.
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in/out; PTT via COM-port keying; CAT rig control for frequency in some builds.
Notes: Long-running product by Mike Bertini; the 'Mscan Meteo' variant is the fax/NAVTEX package.

Name: MultiMode
Function: Decodes many modes on the Mac including SSTV, HF FAX, RTTY, NAVTEX, CW and PSK.
