Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio input from the receiver; primarily receive/decode.
Notes: By Black Cat Systems. A go-to multimode decoder on macOS.

Name: Multiscan / Multiscan 3B
Function: Mac SSTV RX/TX supporting the standard analog modes with templates.
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in/out; serial-port PTT keying.
Notes: By Kok Chen W7AY. Classic Mac OS X SSTV program.

Name: noaa-apt
Function: Decode NOAA APT weather-satellite images from recordings or live audio
Radio Interaction: audio from SDR (offline or piped)
Notes: martinber; modern cross-platform alternative to the listed WXtoImg

Name: PC HF Fax / HF Facsimile
Function: Legacy HF fax and SSTV receive package bundled with simple detector interfaces.
Radio Interaction: Audio via comparator/soundcard interface from the receiver; receive-oriented.
Notes: By Software Systems Consulting (N6RCE); sold with 'HamComm-style' detector hardware in the 1990s.

Name: QSSTV
Function: Receive/transmit analog SSTV, HAMDRM digital SSTV (EasyPal-compatible) and, in recent versions, weather fax; gallery, repeater and template features.
Radio Interaction: PulseAudio/ALSA sound-card in/out; PTT via serial (COM-port) electronic switch; Hamlib CAT radio control.
Notes: By ON4QZ. The standard SSTV app on Linux and Raspberry Pi; interoperates with MMSSTV and EasyPal.

Name: Robot36 - SSTV Image Decoder
Function: Real-time SSTV decoder for Robot, PD, Martin, Scottie and Wraase modes; long popular for decoding ISS/ARISS SSTV events.
Radio Interaction: Decodes audio from the phone mic held to a receiver's speaker, or via an audio cable from the radio; receive-only.
Notes: By Ahmet Inan (xdsopl). A companion 'SSTV Encoder' app handles transmit; also has a browser build.

Name: RX-SSTV
Function: Receive-only SSTV decoder aimed at SWLs and beginners; auto-detects modes, saves images with FTP auto-upload option.
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio input only (decode); no transmit, so no PTT.
Notes: By ON4QZ. Popular lightweight companion to a receiver or scanner.

Name: SatSignal / GeoSatSignal / HRPT Reader
Function: Decode and process APT, HRPT and geostationary weather-satellite imagery
Radio Interaction: audio/receiver or IF input
Notes: David Taylor suite

Name: SDRangel (SSTV/APT/HamDRM demods)
Function: SDR application whose plugins include an SSTV demodulator, APT (weather-satellite) demod and DRM/DSD digital-voice decoders.
Radio Interaction: Controls SDR hardware directly (I/Q, tuning, and TX on capable SDRs); decodes images from the received stream.
Notes: By Edouard Griffiths F4EXB. Convenient one-app path from RF to SSTV/APT images.

Name: SeaTTY
Function: Receives weather charts (HF-FAX/WEFAX), NAVTEX, RTTY, PACTOR-1 FEC and NWR SAME using only a receiver and sound card.
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio input; receive-only (no radio keying).
Notes: By DXsoft (Sergei Podstrigailo). Very popular with boaters for offshore weather.

Name: SkySweeper
Function: Professional/amateur multimode decoder handling WEFAX, NAVTEX, RTTY, SITOR and image modes.
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio input; receive-oriented, with rig-tuning helpers in pro versions.
Notes: Used by hobbyists and monitoring stations for maritime weather and utility modes.

Name: SSTV Encoder
Function: Encodes images to SSTV audio for transmission in many modes; the transmit counterpart to Robot36.
Radio Interaction: Plays SSTV tones from the phone into the radio's mic (acoustic or audio cable); PTT via VOX.
Notes: By xdsopl. Pairs with Robot36 for a full Android SSTV station.

Name: SSTV Slow Scan TV (Black Cat, iOS)
Function: iOS SSTV send/receive app across standard analog modes.
Radio Interaction: Device audio in/out to the radio; PTT via VOX.
Notes: Black Cat Systems iOS SSTV title; often used to grab ISS SSTV passes on a handheld.

Name: W95SSTV
Function: Early Windows sound-card SSTV RX/TX program, predecessor to ChromaPIX.
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in/out; serial-port PTT.
Notes: By Silicon Pixels N7CX. Abandoned but historically important.

Name: Weather Fax 2000
Function: HF radiofax reception and display with scheduling and image processing.
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio input from the receiver; receive-oriented.
Notes: Legacy but still used Windows WEFAX package for marine weather charts.

Name: WEFAX (wojlin)
Function: Open-source tool for decoding and processing HF weather fax (radiofax) from audio/IQ.
Radio Interaction: Takes sound-card/recorded audio from a receiver; decode/receive-oriented.
Notes: Modern GitHub project (wojlin/WEFAX) for scripted/automated fax decoding.

Name: WinPix Pro / WinPix32
Function: Sound-card SSTV send/receive with image editing and mode library.
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in/out; serial-port PTT keying.
Notes: By G.V. Associates (Don Rotolo K0HEO). Legacy Windows SSTV package.

Name: WXsat
Function: Decodes FM facsimile (weather-satellite APT) and HF FAX signals from the sound card.
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio input from the receiver; receive-only.
Notes: By Christian Bock DL9RM. Classic tool bridging HF WEFAX and early satellite decoding.

Name: WXtoImg
Function: Automated NOAA/Meteor APT and WEFAX decoder with recording, map overlays, colour enhancement and 3-D/temperature images.
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio input from an FM receiver/SDR; can trigger recording; receive-only.
Notes: By Craig Anderson. NOAA POES now decommissioned, but still used for archives and Meteor/WEFAX.

Name: APRS-Beacon
Function: APRS beaconing/tracking on up to four radio ports
Radio Interaction: Single-port TNC (native), single/dual-port TNC in KISS, or AGW Packet Engine; PTT via TNC
Notes: Multi-radio APRS transmit tool.

Name: Argo
Function: High-resolution spectrogram decoder for very slow CW / QRSS, DFCW and MTHell weak-signal LF/MF beacon modes
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio input from a receiver (receive/visual decode only)
Notes: By Alberto di Bene I2PHD; classic QRSS visual weak-signal RX tool.

Name: Decodium
Function: WSJT-X-derived program built around the fast FT2 mode for rapid digital QSOs (also FT8/FT4)
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control, sound-card audio, PTT keying (WSJT-X lineage)
Notes: Niche fast-mode experiment in the FT-family; often paired with DecoAlert.

Name: DigiRite
Function: Multi-stream FT8/FT4 contesting front-end integrated with WriteLog
Radio Interaction: rig control + audio via WriteLog
Notes: Kevin Schmidt W9CF; the WriteLog counterpart to N1MM's FT8 support

Name: DigiSkimmer
Function: Multi-band FT8/FT4/WSPR skimmer that decodes many bands at once from KiwiSDR receivers and reports spots
Radio Interaction: Connects to and tunes networked KiwiSDR receivers (RX only) to pull audio/IQ
Notes: Used to build wide-coverage digital-mode spotting stations.

Name: Faros
Function: Automated NCDXF/IARU HF beacon monitoring; measures SNR, path delay and QSB, plots propagation
Radio Interaction: audio from RX + CAT rig control (auto-cycles bands via Omni-Rig)
Notes: Alex Shovkoplyas VE3NEA (DX Atlas suite); companion to Rocky/CW Skimmer

Name: FT Ham (FT8/FT4 transceiver)
Function: Complete FT8/FT4 decoder and audio encoder/transmitter with live waterfall, auto QSO sequencing and ADIF logging, fully offline
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in/out via device audio or a connected radio interface (mic/speaker or line); UTC-aligned TX slots
Notes: Requires iOS 16.6+/macOS 13.5+ (Apple-silicon Mac).

Name: FT8 Decoder (Bunzee)
Function: Decodes FT8 traffic in real time from externally provided audio, showing callsigns, reports and SNR
Radio Interaction: Sound-card/audio input from an SSB or SDR receiver (receive-only; no CAT/TX)
Notes: Same developer as FT8 Radio; also a related 'ft8d' command-line decoder exists.

Name: FT8 Radio (Bunzee)
Function: Receive-and-transmit FT8 app aimed at portable operation
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio to/from the rig via an interface (or acoustic/VOX); works with an external SSB/SDR radio
Notes: By the Bunzee/DigitalRadioReceiver developer; companion to their FT8 Decoder app.

Name: FT8AF
Function: Native Android FT8/FT4/FT2 app: decode the band, full auto-sequence, map QSO paths, POTA-oriented workflow
Radio Interaction: Drives the radio over USB CAT; sound-card audio via USB interface or device audio
Notes: Positioned as a native, portable FT8 station for phones/tablets.

Name: FT8CN
Function: Full FT8 (and FT4) decode/transmit application for phones and tablets with logging and QSO auto-sequencing
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control over USB-serial, Bluetooth or WiFi/network; audio via device mic/speaker (VOX) or a USB-C sound interface (e.g. DigiRig); PTT via CAT
Notes: By BG7YOZ (mirrored by N0BOY); the most popular way to run FT8 from an Android device with a real radio.

Name: ft8modem
Function: Command-line software modem for FT8 exposing a simple interface for scripted/automated FT8 TX and RX
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in/out and PTT keying to a connected transceiver
Notes: By KK5JY; used to build custom/automated FT8 stations.

Name: ft8mon
Function: Command-line FT8 monitor/decoder that prints decodes from a live audio or SDR stream
Radio Interaction: Reads sound-card audio or an SDR (e.g. RTL-SDR/receiver) input for decoding (RX)
Notes: By Robert Morris AB1HL; its decoder code is reused in several other FT8 projects.

Name: GridTracker
Function: Visual companion for WSJT-X/JS8Call showing worked grids/spots on a map and providing logging and rig actions.
Radio Interaction: Reads/sends UDP to the digital engine and can control the radio via Hamlib rigctld to change frequency/band from the map.
Notes: The classic FT8 map/companion with optional direct Hamlib rig control.

Name: GridTracker2
Function: Rewritten next-generation GridTracker with expanded mapping, logging and station-control features.
Radio Interaction: Same UDP bridge to WSJT-X plus Hamlib/rigctld control to tune the transceiver from spots.
Notes: Successor to GridTracker; modernized UI and integrations.

Name: JS8Call
Function: Keyboard-to-keyboard weak-signal messaging using the JS8 protocol (derived from FT8) — real-time chat, relays, store-and-forward, APRS gateway
Radio Interaction: Hamlib CAT rig control, sound-card audio in/out, PTT via CAT/RTS/DTR/VOX
Notes: By Jordan KN4CRD; originally 'FT8Call'. Widely used for EmComm/off-grid text over HF.

Name: JT65-HF
Function: Original standalone transmit/receive of JT65A weak-signal mode on HF
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control + audio (sound card) + PTT
Notes: Joe Large W6CQZ; the program that popularized JT65 on HF before WSJT-X; historically significant, still referenced

Name: JT65-HF HB9HQX Edition
Function: Enhanced JT65-HF fork with jt65v2 protocol, multi-decoder and logger integrations
Radio Interaction: CAT + audio + PTT
Notes: Beat Oehrli HB9HQX fork; logs to DXKeeper/HRD/JT65-Log; distinct from the W6CQZ original

Name: JTAlert
Function: Alerting/decode-management companion to WSJT-X/JTDX that adds callsign alerts, logging bridges and rig-frequency actions.
Radio Interaction: Bridges to the radio through WSJT-X (which controls the rig via Hamlib/OmniRig); can trigger frequency/mode changes and works with DXLab Commander for CAT.
Notes: By Laurie Cowcher VK3AMA; touches the rig indirectly via the digital-mode engine.

Name: JTDX
Function: DX/weak-signal-optimized WSJT-X fork for FT8, FT4, JT9, JT65, T10; tuned decoder, filters and call/CQ automation
Radio Interaction: Hamlib CAT rig control, sound-card audio in/out, PTT via CAT/RTS/DTR/VOX
Notes: By Igor UA3DJY and team; favored by contesters and extreme weak-signal DXers.

Name: JTDX Improved
Function: Community fork of JTDX with additional decode/UI tweaks and mode support
Radio Interaction: Same as JTDX — Hamlib CAT, sound-card audio, PTT keying
Notes: Appears in decode-comparison tests alongside WSJT-X and MSHV.

Name: Linrad
Function: Software-defined-radio DSP receiver optimized for weak-signal work (EME, tropo), noise blanking, adaptive filtering; feeds MAP65
Radio Interaction: Drives/receives IQ from SDR front-ends and sound cards; provides receiver tuning/hardware control
Notes: By Leif SM5BSZ; classic weak-signal SDR paired with MAP65 for EME.

Name: MAP65
Function: Wideband adaptive-polarization decoder for JT65 and Q65 EME — decodes every signal in up to a ~90 kHz passband and builds a band map; combines orthogonal (H/V) antennas
Radio Interaction: Takes wideband IQ from SDR-style hardware (via Linrad/network); controls receiver tuning; RX-centric with WSJT-X for TX
Notes: By K1JT; the standard 2 m/VHF EME wideband tool, typically run with Linrad.

Name: MSHV
Function: Multimode weak-signal app: FT8, FT4, JT65, JT9, PI4 plus meteor-scatter/MS modes MSK144, MSKMS, JTMS, FSK441, FSK315, ISCAT, JT6M; Multi-Answering auto-sequence for DXpeditions
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control (Hamlib), sound-card audio in/out, PTT via CAT/serial/VOX
Notes: By Christo LZ2HV; the go-to for multi-stream/Fox-style DXpedition and lightweight/portable setups.

Name: pico_ft8_xcvr
Function: Standalone real-time FT8 transceiver firmware running FT8 decode and transmit on a Pi Pico
Radio Interaction: On-board DSP generates/decodes FT8 audio and keys a small RF front-end — a self-contained FT8 radio
Notes: Fork by kholia of AA1GD's project; demonstrates FT8 on a microcontroller (akin to PixelFT8-style builds).

Name: PropNetPSK
Function: Automated PSK propagation-beacon client that transmits and multi-decodes formatted beacons to report real-time HF propagation
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in/out, CAT rig control, and PTT keying to transmit beacons
Notes: Client for the PropNET RF propagation-reporting network; actively keys the rig, so not a pure reporting tool.

Name: QMAP
Function: Single-channel wideband receive-only decoder for all 30 s/60 s Q65 submodes, derived from MAP65
Radio Interaction: Wideband SDR IQ input; receive-only, cooperates with WSJT-X which handles CAT/TX
Notes: By K1JT; bundled with recent WSJT-X for EME Q65 operation.

Name: ROS
Function: Weak-signal MFSK chat/beacon modes (ROS, ROS-EME/MF variants) for HF/VHF
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in/out for TX/RX and PTT keying via serial/VOX
Notes: By José Alberto Nieto Ros EA5HVK; a legacy weak-signal digimode with a dedicated program.

Name: rpitx
Function: Transmit FM/AM/SSB/SSTV/FSQ/POCSAG/WSPR/chirp directly from a Raspberry Pi GPIO pin
Radio Interaction: direct RF synthesis on GPIO18 (the Pi itself is the transmitter)
Notes: F5OEO; broader than the listed WsprryPi (which only does WSPR); needs filtering

Name: rtlsdr-ft8d
Function: Standalone FT8 receiver/decoder that captures directly from an RTL-SDR for VHF/UHF FT8 monitoring/reporting
Radio Interaction: Directly tunes and reads IQ from RTL-SDR hardware (RX only)
Notes: By Guenael VA2GKA; companion to rtlsdr-wsprd (project noted as stalled but still in use).

Name: rtlsdr-wsprd
Function: Lightweight daemon that receives and decodes WSPR directly from an RTL-SDR dongle and auto-reports spots to WSPRnet
Radio Interaction: Directly tunes and reads IQ from RTL-SDR hardware (no sound card needed)
Notes: By Guenael VA2GKA; popular for cheap always-on WSPR receive beacons.

Name: SDR++ Brown
Function: Feature-extended fork of SDR++ that adds an FT8/FT4 decoder and other DXpedition-oriented tools
Radio Interaction: Directly controls SDR hardware (RTL-SDR, Airspy, HackRF, SDRplay, etc.) and decodes FT8 from IQ
Notes: By 'sannysanoff'; brings on-band FT8 decoding into the SDR++ waterfall.

Name: SDR-Control for Icom
Function: Operate networked Icom SDR radios (IC-705/7300/7610/9700/7760 etc.) with waterfall, satellite mode and an integrated FT8/FT4 tool
Radio Interaction: Full network (LAN/Internet) CAT rig control and streamed radio audio to the Icom; FT8/FT4 handled internally — no cables/audio drivers
Notes: By Marcus Roskosch DL8MRE; iPad version adds waterfall/satellite features. Multi-pass FT8 decoding.

Name: SparkSDR
Platforms: Windows / macOS / Linux / Linux ARM (Raspberry Pi)
Function: Cross-platform SDR transceiver app for HPSDR/Hermes-Lite 2/SDRplay with built-in FT8/FT4 decode on multiple virtual receivers (monitor/decode 4–11 frequencies at once)
Radio Interaction: Directly controls the SDR hardware (tuning, TX on Hermes-Lite) and decodes/encodes FT8 internally — no virtual audio or CAT cables needed
Notes: Popular Hermes-Lite 2 companion; simultaneous multi-band FT8 without WSJT-X.

Name: Spectran
Function: Real-time audio spectrum/waterfall analyzer for weak-signal & QRSS reception
Radio Interaction: audio (sound card) from receiver
Notes: Alberto di Bene I2PHD; direct forerunner of Argo (which is listed)

Name: Spectrum Lab
Function: Highly configurable audio spectrum analyzer/DSP for QRSS, EME, WSPR calibration, EbNaut, DCF77
Radio Interaction: audio (sound card) in/out; optional CAT + PTT; frequency calibration
Notes: Wolfgang Buescher DL4YHF; ubiquitous in weak-signal and QRSS work

Name: weakmon
Function: Python sound-card programs to receive and decode FT8, JT65 and WSPR (plus WWV utilities)
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio input from an SSB/SDR receiver; optional Hamlib CAT for band control (RX-focused)
Notes: By Robert Morris AB1HL; reference/experimental decoders.

Name: WOLF / WOLF GUI
Function: Weak-signal Operating on Ludicrously Low Frequencies — extremely-low-SNR coherent beacon/QSO mode with a GUI front-end
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in/out and PTT keying to an LF transmitter
Notes: Legacy LF experimental mode still referenced in weak-signal software catalogs.

Name: WSJT (original)
Function: The original WSJT program (2001+) for meteor scatter and EME: FSK441, JT6M, JT4, JT44, JT65
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in/out for TX/RX, PTT keying via serial/VOX, basic CAT
Notes: Legacy K1JT program still used for VHF meteor scatter/EME; predecessor to WSJT-X.

Name: WSJT-X
Function: Flagship weak-signal suite implementing FT8, FT4, JT65, JT9, JT4, Q65, FST4, FST4W, MSK144 (meteor scatter), WSPR and Echo (EME); includes Fox/Hound and SuperFox DXpedition modes
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control via Hamlib (frequency/mode/split), sound-card audio in/out for TX/RX, PTT keying via CAT/RTS/DTR/VOX
Notes: By Joe Taylor K1JT and the WSJT Development Team; the reference implementation nearly every other FT8 program forks or interoperates with (UDP telemetry).

Name: WSJT-X Improved
Function: Fork of WSJT-X adding extra features: multi-decoder, extended logging, message/color enhancements, FT2 support
Radio Interaction: Same as WSJT-X — Hamlib CAT, sound-card audio, PTT via CAT/serial/VOX
Notes: Maintained by Uwe DG2YCB; popular among contesters/DXers for its improved decode counts.

Name: WSJT-Z
Function: Automation-focused WSJT-X fork: Auto-CQ/Auto-Call, DXCC/prefix/state/continent/worked-before filters, band hopping, QRZ lookup, multi-threaded FT8 decoder (FT8/FT4/FT2/JT*/WSPR)
Radio Interaction: Hamlib CAT rig control, sound-card audio, PTT keying; can drive the transceiver fully automatically
Notes: By SQ9FVE; releases on GitHub/SourceForge. 'Robot' full-auto operation — user must monitor the rig.

Name: WSPR (standalone)
Function: Original standalone WSPR program (v2.x) for Weak Signal Propagation Reporter beaconing and reception on LF–UHF
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control for band scheduling, sound-card audio in/out, PTT keying; auto-uploads spots to WSPRnet
Notes: By K1JT; WSPR functionality later folded into WSJT-X but the standalone is still used.

Name: WSPR-X
Function: Experimental WSPR variant adding the slow WSPR-15 sub-mode for extreme weak-signal/LF propagation testing
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control, sound-card audio, PTT keying; WSPRnet reporting
Notes: By K1JT; legacy experimental branch.

Name: wsprd
Function: Command-line WSPR decoder (the decode engine behind WSPR/WSJT-X), used in headless RX/reporting stations
Radio Interaction: Decodes a sound-card or SDR-provided audio/IQ stream from a receiver (RX side)
Notes: Frequently scripted with SDR captures to build unattended WSPR monitors.

Name: wsprdaemon (WD)
Function: Robust service decoding WSPR and all FST4W variants from one or many SDRs simultaneously and posting to WSPRnet, with extra noise/Doppler metrics
Radio Interaction: Controls and reads IQ from KiwiSDR, RX888, RTL-SDR and other SDRs (multi-band/multi-receiver RX)
Notes: By Rob Robinett AI6VN; the top WSPRnet spotters collectively report ~a third of daily spots via WD.

Name: WsprryPi
Function: Turns a Raspberry Pi into a standalone WSPR transmitter, generating RF on a GPIO pin with NTP frequency calibration
Radio Interaction: The Pi IS the transmitter — synthesizes/keys the WSPR RF signal directly on GPIO (requires external low-pass filter)
Notes: 2.x rewrite maintained by lbussy (orig. JamesP6000); classic no-radio-needed QRP beacon.

Name: WSQCall / WSQ2
Function: Weak Signal QSO keyboard mode for LF/MF (630 m/2200 m) real-time text at very low SNR
Radio Interaction: Sound-card audio in/out and PTT keying to the transceiver
Notes: By Con Wassilieff ZL2AFP; niche low-band weak-signal chat.

Name: AirMail
Function: Long-running Winlink and maritime HF email client with terminal window and PACTOR support
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control + PTT keying; drives SCS PACTOR modems and sound-card modes
Notes: Also the client used by the SailMail maritime service; predates Winlink Express.

Name: Flarq
Function: ARQ (automatic-repeat-request) file and email transfer engine
Radio Interaction: Uses fldigi as the on-air modem (sound-card + PTT)
Notes: Provides reliable point-to-point transfers over fldigi modes

Name: ICOM Marine Radio Control Program
Function: Simplifies control of Icom HF SSB marine radios for Winlink/PACTOR email operation
Radio Interaction: CAT/CI-V serial rig control (frequency, mode) of Icom marine SSB sets
Notes: Listed on the Winlink client-software page as an accessory for marine HF stations.

Name: JNOS 2.0
Function: KA9Q-NOS derived TCP/IP-over-radio stack with packet BBS and Winlink gateway/forwarding
Radio Interaction: KISS/AX.25 TNC audio + PTT (sound modem or hardware TNC)
Notes: Long-standing EmComm/packet server platform.

Name: LinBPQ / BPQ32
Function: AX.25 NET/ROM node, packet BBS, chat server, RMS gateway and Winlink client suite
Radio Interaction: KISS/JKISS/hardware TNCs and sound modems; supports PACTOR, WINMOR, ARDOP and VARA data-over-radio; keys PTT
Notes: By John Wiseman G8BPQ (cantab.net). Backbone of many RMS gateways and packet networks.

Name: Outpost Packet Message Manager
Function: Emergency packet messaging client for BBS/Winlink store-and-forward traffic handling
Radio Interaction: Controls AX.25 packet TNCs / KISS soundmodem; PTT via TNC
Notes: Widely used by ARES/RACES for packet message handling over VHF/UHF radios.

Name: Paclink
Function: Streamlined Winlink client with built-in POP3/SMTP server so any standard email program can send/receive over radio; single- or multi-user
Radio Interaction: Drives packet TNC / sound modem and HF modems for the radio link; PTT via TNC/CAT
Notes: Lets you use Outlook/Thunderbird etc. as the Winlink front end.

Name: Paclink-unix
Function: UNIX/Linux Winlink client exposing POP/SMTP so local mail clients compose/read Winlink messages
Radio Interaction: AX.25/KISS packet TNC, ARDOP and PACTOR modems; PTT via TNC/Hamlib
Notes: github.com/nwdigitalradio/paclink-unix.

Name: Pat
Platforms: Linux / macOS / Windows / BSD / Raspberry Pi (web GUI + CLI)
Function: Modern cross-platform Winlink client in Go with browser GUI and command line
Radio Interaction: Rig control via Hamlib/rigctl (freq/PTT); connects to ARDOP and VARA HF/FM over TCP, AX.25/KISS for packet, telnet for internet
Notes: getpat.io / github.com/la5nta/pat. Popular on Raspberry Pi gateways and headless stations.

Name: PSKmail
Function: Server/client for email, chat and position reporting over PSK, using fldigi as the on-air modem
Radio Interaction: Drives fldigi (sound-card audio + PTT) to key the radio
Notes: ARQ email system built on the fldigi modem; overlaps with Winlink-style messaging

Name: RadioMail
Function: First native iOS Winlink client: telnet + over-the-air email, 100+ standard forms, GPS-prefilled forms, image optimization
Radio Interaction: Pairs with external modems (e.g. VARA over network, PACTOR) and controls the rig for HF/VHF Winlink sessions
Notes: radiomail.app. Also supports maritime/HF email workflows.

Name: RMS Packet
Function: VHF/UHF packet gateway station into the Winlink (WL2K) network; also VARA FM
Radio Interaction: KISS TNC or soundmodem; PTT via TNC
Notes: Server-side Winlink RMS software listening for AX.25/VARA FM.

Name: RMS Relay
Function: Local store-and-forward message database for RMS gateways; buffers when internet is lost and enables the Hybrid HF radio network
Radio Interaction: Works with RMS Packet/RMS Trimode which handle the actual radio link (audio/PTT/CAT)
Notes: Enables internet-independent message forwarding between HF gateways.

Name: RMS Trimode
Function: HF gateway server supporting PACTOR (P1-P4), WINMOR, Robust Packet, ARDOP and VARA HF on one radio
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control + PTT; interfaces SCS PACTOR modems and sound-card modems (ARDOP/VARA/WINMOR)
Notes: Replaces the deprecated RMS Pactor and RMS WINMOR; can pair with RMS Relay for the Hybrid network.

Name: SailMail (AirMail)
Function: Subscription HF radio-email network for boaters using the AirMail client and Winlink-derived technology
Radio Interaction: Same as AirMail: CAT rig control, PTT, SCS PACTOR / sound-card modem over marine SSB
Notes: No amateur license required; runs on marine/ham SSB. Built on Winlink software lineage.

Name: SCS PACTOR software (PTC / P4dragon tools & WA8DED terminal)
Function: Terminal and firmware-update utilities for SCS PTC-IIIusb and P4dragon DR-7800 Pactor modems
Radio Interaction: SCS hardware Pactor modem over USB/serial; CAT + PTT via the modem
Notes: Pactor 1-4 and Robust Packet; used with Winlink Express and Airmail.

Name: SCS Trimode
Function: SCS's own multimode terminal enabling PACTOR/Robust Packet/RTTY operation with SCS PTC/Dragon modems
Radio Interaction: Controls the SCS hardware PACTOR modem which handles audio; keys PTT and rig via the modem
Notes: Distinct from Winlink's RMS Trimode; the vendor host for SCS PACTOR controllers.

Name: Telpac
Function: Early Winlink 2000 telnet-packet gateway node front end linking VHF packet users to a PMBO/CMS
Radio Interaction: Packet TNC for the RF side (AX.25 audio + PTT)
Notes: Superseded by RMS Packet but historically important.

Name: Winlink Express (RMS Express)
Function: Flagship full-featured Winlink radio-email client: telnet, packet, HF, P2P, standard forms, message management
Radio Interaction: CAT rig control (frequency/mode) + sound-card audio in/out; keys PTT and drives bundled/external modems (ARDOP TNC, WINMOR TNC, VARA HF/FM, Packet via UZ7HO/Direwolf, SCS PACTOR)
Notes: The reference Winlink client; supports the most modes. Registration key requested via a one-time radio message.

Name: WoAD (Winlink on Android)
Function: Mobile Winlink client with full standard-forms support, telnet and over-the-air sessions
Radio Interaction: Connects over Bluetooth/USB/TCP to VARA HF/FM, ARDOP and KISS packet TNCs; PTT handled by the paired modem
Notes: Widely used for go-kits; pairs with a phone/tablet + external soft-TNC.
